slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise overview of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric implementation, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and its intended audience.

## 2.1 Project Overview

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a unified SaaS experience. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards.

UMS is undertaking this project to design and implement an end-to-end data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project scope includes the following high-level objectives:
- Establishment of a Microsoft Fabric workspace tailored to UMS’s business needs.
- Development of a comprehensive ETL pipeline for data ingestion, transformation, and orchestration using Fabric Data Factory.
- Enablement of advanced analytics and big data processing through Fabric Synapse.
- Integration of Machine Learning processes via Fabric Data Science.
- Delivery of business-aligned analytics and visualization through Power BI.

This initiative will support UMS’s cloud adoption strategy by leveraging the full capabilities of Microsoft Fabric’s services for data integration, analytics, and machine learning.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

A Solution Design Document is a formal artifact that outlines the technical architecture, design decisions, and implementation plan for a technology project. The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to provide a comprehensive and authoritative reference for the design, deployment, and governance of the Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution for UMS. It serves as the primary document for technical alignment between Enfrasys Solutions and UMS, and will be used for review, validation, and formal sign-off prior to implementation.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document. UMS shall review, provide feedback, and formally sign off on the finalized Solution Design Document.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The Solution Design Document is intended for the following stakeholders:
- UMS IT Department
- UMS Business Stakeholders
- Enfrasys Solutions Architects and Consultants

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing and maintaining this document. UMS is responsible for reviewing the content, ensuring alignment with business and technical requirements, and providing final approval and sign-off.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview

This section outlines the foundational Microsoft technologies that underpin the UMS data workflow solution, including Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. Fabric consolidates core services such as Data Factory, Synapse, Data Science, and OneLake to provide a seamless end-to-end data experience.

- **Data Factory**: Enables data integration and orchestration, allowing organizations to ingest, prepare, and transform data from diverse sources.
- **Synapse**: Delivers advanced data analytics and big data processing, supporting both serverless and provisioned compute options for large-scale analytics workloads.
- **Data Science**: Provides an environment for developing, training, and operationalizing machine learning models, including AutoML capabilities.
- **OneLake**: Acts as a unified data lake storage layer, ensuring secure, scalable, and centralized data storage across all Fabric workloads.

Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric serves as the backbone for establishing a secure workspace, developing end-to-end ETL pipelines, conducting machine learning processes, and integrating with Power BI for business-aligned analytics.

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business analytics platform that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making across organizations. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing seamless access to data stored in OneLake and processed via Data Factory and Synapse.

Power BI’s core analytics capabilities include:
- Interactive dashboards and reports
- Real-time data visualization
- Natural language query support
- Deep integration with Microsoft 365 and Azure services

In the UMS solution, Power BI will be leveraged to deliver business-aligned analytics, enabling stakeholders to consume actionable insights derived from the end-to-end data workflow orchestrated within Microsoft Fabric.

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides secure authentication, single sign-on (SSO), and role-based access control (RBAC) for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft cloud services.

Entra ID is essential for:
- Authenticating UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI
- Enforcing security policies and conditional access
- Managing workspace-level roles and permissions

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for providing the technical content and configuration guidance for Entra ID integration. UMS shall validate and approve all authentication and access requirements prior to implementation.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture for UMS, focusing on secure connectivity between on-premises resources and Microsoft Fabric, including the deployment of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) and associated security controls.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

UMS's hybrid data architecture requires secure, reliable connectivity between the on-premises Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric cloud services. Data flows originate from the UMS staging area, traverse through the Data Gateway, and are ingested into the cloud for processing and analytics. All data transfers are secured via outbound connections, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules and minimizing the attack surface.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_009_img_003.png)

Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

### Definition: Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway

The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure, lightweight bridge that enables cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Logic Apps to securely access data residing on-premises. The gateway establishes outbound encrypted connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints, ensuring data remains protected during transit and that no inbound ports need to be opened on the corporate firewall.

### OPDG Design for UMS

Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine within the UMS on-premises environment. This VM will serve as the exclusive conduit for data transfers between the UMS SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric, adhering to Microsoft's security and performance best practices.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_011_img_004.png)

Figure 2: Secure data gateway connectivity between on-premises SQL Server and UMS Fabric.

### VM Specification for OPDG

| Component         | Specification                                       |
|-------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)|
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                        |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                            |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                       |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and configuration of the OPDG and associated network controls. UMS shall ensure the readiness of on-premises resources, including VM provisioning and network prerequisites.

## 4.3 Outbound Security Rules for Data Gateway

To maintain secure and compliant operations, the following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) outbound rules must be implemented for the OPDG VM. These rules ensure the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data with Microsoft Fabric and related services.

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net              | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                                           | 443              | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 80               | TCP      | Allow  |

Enfrasys Solutions will design and document these security rules. UMS is responsible for implementing the rules within their on-premises or Azure-hosted network environment.

| Network Architecture Design  | Develop overall network topology and data flow diagrams                                                 | Enfrasys Solutions  | Open   |
| OPDG VM Specification        | Define and document VM requirements for OPDG                                                            | Enfrasys Solutions  | Open   |
| OPDG VM Provisioning         | Provision VM according to specification and ensure OS/.NET prerequisites                               | UMS                 | Open   |
| OPDG Agent Deployment        | Install and configure the On-Premises Data Gateway agent                                                | Enfrasys Solutions  | Open   |
| Outbound NSG Rule Design     | Define and document outbound NSG rules for secure gateway operation                                     | Enfrasys Solutions  | Open   |
| Outbound NSG Rule Implementation | Apply outbound NSG rules to the OPDG VM/network interface                                             | UMS                 | Open   |
| Network Connectivity Testing | Validate secure connectivity from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric and related endpoints                     | Enfrasys Solutions  | Open   |
| On-Premises Resource Readiness| Ensure SQL Server and network infrastructure are accessible and meet gateway requirements               | UMS                 | Open   |

## 4.5 Network Design Considerations

| ID | Description                                                                                                         | Workload Type      |
|----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| 1  | All data transfers between on-premises and Fabric must use outbound, encrypted connections via the OPDG             | Data Integration   |

## 4.6 Network Design Decisions

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access mappings for Microsoft Fabric within the UMS environment.

## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform from Microsoft, integrating data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a unified SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged position responsible for the governance, configuration, and operational management of the Microsoft Fabric environment.

Administrators have the following capabilities:

| Capability | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Environment Configuration | Manage tenant-level settings, enable/disable features, and configure integration with other Microsoft services. |
| Security Management | Assign and manage workspace roles, enforce data access policies, and monitor security events. |
| Resource Provisioning | Create and manage workspaces, assign capacities, and oversee resource usage. |
| Audit and Compliance | Access audit logs, monitor usage, and ensure compliance with organizational and regulatory requirements. |
| Support and Troubleshooting | Address platform issues, liaise with Microsoft support, and coordinate incident response. |

Enfrasys Solutions shall define the Fabric Administrator role and its associated permissions. UMS shall assign qualified personnel to this role and manage ongoing user access.

## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles

Microsoft Fabric provides several native roles to support various analytics and data operations. These roles are summarized below:

| Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Manage workspace settings | ✔ |         |        |        |
| Add/remove users          | ✔ |         |        |        |
| Create/edit/delete content| ✔ | ✔       | ✔      |        |
| Publish datasets/reports  | ✔ | ✔       | ✔      |        |
| View content              | ✔ | ✔       | ✔      | ✔      |
| Share content             | ✔ | ✔       | ✔      |        |
| Schedule refreshes        | ✔ | ✔       |        |        |
| Assign roles              | ✔ |         |        |        |

**Role Descriptions:**

- **Admin:** Full control over workspace and content; can manage users and settings.
- **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and publish content; cannot manage workspace settings or users.
- **Member:** Can interact with and view content; limited publishing/editing rights.
- **Viewer:** Read-only access to workspace content.

Enfrasys Solutions shall define and document the native roles. UMS is responsible for assigning users to these roles as per operational needs.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision

This section documents the recommended Microsoft Fabric architecture, workflow, access, and governance design for UMS, including key technical decisions and responsibilities.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It provides services such as Data Factory for data integration, Synapse for advanced analytics, and Power BI for visualization, all operating over OneLake, a unified data lake.

The following table summarizes the primary workflow considerations for UMS’s Fabric deployment:

| ID    | Description                                                                                                  | Workload Type      |
|-------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| DC01  | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                         | Data Factory       |
| DC02  | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                        | Data Engineering   |
| DC03  | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                             | Data Engineering   |
| DC04  | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                            | Data Engineering   |
| DC05  | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering   |
| DC06  | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).  | Data Factory       |
| DC07  | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science       |
| DC08  | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                 | Power BI           |
| DC09  | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                      | Power BI           |
| DE01  | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering   |
| DE02  | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python). | Data Science   |
| DE03  | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment.         | Data Science       |
| DE04  | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry.                                              | Data Science       |
| DE05  | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data.                                | Data Engineering   |
| DE06  | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results.              | Data Engineering   |
| DE07  | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI     |
| DE08  | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                      | Power BI           |

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_009_img_003.png)
Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture from ingestion to Power BI.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_021_img_005.png)
Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow leveraging AutoML and Notebooks in Microsoft Fabric.

## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions

The following table documents the key design decisions for each workflow consideration:

| No.  | Design Decision                                                                                   | Decision                                                                                   |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DD01 | Data ingestion shall use Data Factory pipelines to extract data from Microsoft SQL Server.        | Adopt Fabric Data Factory pipelines for all batch and scheduled ingestion.                 |
| DD02 | Lakehouse architecture with Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers shall be implemented.                 | Enforce multi-layered Lakehouse for data quality, traceability, and reusability.           |
| DD03 | All data cleansing and transformation shall be performed using Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks.       | Use Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks for ETL and advanced data prep.                            |
| DD04 | Machine learning experimentation shall be performed on Silver Lakehouse data.                     | Use Silver layer as the trusted input for ML processes.                                    |
| DD05 | Manual ML and AutoML shall be supported, with models registered in Fabric Model Registry.         | Enable both ML approaches for flexibility and governance.                                   |
| DD06 | Scored outputs and predictions shall be stored in the Gold Lakehouse.                             | Standardize Gold layer as the source for reporting and analytics.                          |
| DD07 | Power BI Semantic Models shall connect via OneLake Direct Lake mode.                              | Optimize for performance and data freshness in reporting.                                   |
| DD08 | Power BI Desktop shall be used for report development and visualization.                          | Empower business users with self-service analytics.                                        |

## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions

### 6.3.1 Conditional Access

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is a policy engine in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) that evaluates signals such as user, location, device compliance, and risk to enforce access controls for applications and resources.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_006_img_002.png)
Figure 3: Entra Conditional Access policy flow for Microsoft Fabric.

| No.  | Policy                                                                                                    | Decision                                                                                  |
|------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Not implemented in current phase due to licensing; revisit upon Entra P1/P2 acquisition.  |
| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                         | Not implemented; MFA enforced at tenant level as interim control.                         |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                     | Enforced; MFA is mandatory for all UMS users.                                             |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                       | Allowed via Entra ID authentication and MFA.                                              |

### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is a security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to gain access to resources, significantly reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

**Design Decision:**  
UMS shall enforce MFA at the tenant level for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and related services. This is implemented as a compensating control due to the absence of Entra ID P1/P2 licenses for Conditional Access.

### 6.3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and directory services for Microsoft 365, Azure, and thousands of other SaaS applications.

**Design Decision:**  
All user authentication to Microsoft Fabric shall be managed by Microsoft Entra ID. UMS is responsible for user lifecycle management, group assignment, and periodic access reviews.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_006_img_001.png)
Figure 4: Conditional Access enforcement for UMS Fabric access.

Figure 5: Data Gateway integration between UMS SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric.

## 6.5 Resource Organization Design

### 6.5.1 Management Groups

Enfrasys recommends that UMS organize Azure resources using a hierarchical management group structure. This enables centralized policy enforcement, cost management, and resource governance across environments (e.g., Production, Development, Test). The Fabric resource group (Fabric-RG) shall be nested under the appropriate management group for analytics workloads.

### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging

Consistent naming and tagging standards are essential for resource traceability, automation, and cost allocation.

| Storage Account | st          | umsanalytstg01        |

| 4   | Dataflow          | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF<br>StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF<br>Etc.. |

| PL      | Pipeline                  |
| WS      | Workspace                 |

## 6.6 Governance Consideration

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security architecture, network rules, and encryption approaches for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric deployment.

## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that enables administrators to control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources at the subnet and network interface level. NSGs use rules to permit or deny traffic based on source/destination, port, and protocol.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following NSG outbound rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM to ensure secure, compliant connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and implementation of these rules. UMS shall monitor compliance and enforce ongoing operational governance.

### Outbound NSG Rules for Data Gateway VM

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net                                       | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                             | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is fundamental to protecting data confidentiality and integrity across all UMS workloads. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the security architecture and technical configuration. UMS is responsible for monitoring compliance with all regulatory and organizational requirements.

### Encryption at Rest

- **Microsoft Fabric**: All data stored in Microsoft Fabric (including OneLake and Fabric Data Warehouses) is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys with Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE).
- **Power BI**: Power BI datasets and reports are encrypted at rest with Microsoft-managed keys. Customers may optionally configure Power BI to use customer-managed keys (CMK) for enhanced control.
- **SQL Server**: For Azure SQL Database, Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is enabled by default, ensuring all data files, backups, and transaction logs are encrypted at rest.
- **On-Premises Data Gateway**: The gateway uses Windows encryption mechanisms to protect credentials and sensitive configuration data at rest.

### Encryption in Transit

- **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI**: All data transmitted between clients, services, and storage is encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
- **SQL Server**: Connections to Azure SQL Database are secured with TLS encryption by default.
- **On-Premises Data Gateway**: The gateway establishes outbound HTTPS (TLS) connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints, ensuring secure data transfer.

### Encryption Design Table

| No. | Data Flow/Component         | Encryption at Rest          | Encryption in Transit     |
|-----|----------------------------|-----------------------------|--------------------------|
| 1   | Microsoft Fabric (OneLake)  | Azure SSE (Microsoft Keys)  | TLS 1.2+                 |
| 2   | Power BI                   | SSE / Optional CMK          | TLS 1.2+                 |
| 3   | Azure SQL Database         | TDE (Microsoft Keys)        | TLS 1.2+                 |
| 4   | On-Premises Data Gateway   | Windows Encryption          | TLS 1.2+ (HTTPS)         |

## 7.3 Conditional Access and MFA Enforcement

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing secure authentication, conditional access, and identity protection for cloud resources.

UMS’s current licensing level does not support Conditional Access policies. As a compensating control, Enfrasys Solutions has enforced Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users to reduce unauthorized access risk. Conditional Access may be revisited if UMS upgrades to Entra ID P1 or P2 in the future.

### Conditional Access and MFA Design Decisions

| No.  | Design Decision                                                                                      | Decision                    |
|------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Not currently supported     |
| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                   | Not currently supported     |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts               | Enforced at tenant level    |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                 | Supported with MFA          |

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9f576fa6-dfa4-4faa-b281-e4c97902cef8\images\slide_006_img_002.png)

Figure 1: Conditional Access and authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric.

## 7.4 Security Task Assignment

| Item                      | Activities                                                                                              | Action By            | Status   |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------|----------|
| NSG Rule Implementation   | Design and implement outbound NSG rules for Data Gateway VM                                             | Enfrasys Solutions   | Planned  |
| Encryption Configuration  | Validate encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows                                           | Enfrasys Solutions   | Planned  |
| MFA Enforcement           | Enforce MFA for all UMS users at tenant level                                                           | Enfrasys Solutions   | Complete |
| Compliance Monitoring     | Monitor ongoing compliance with security and encryption requirements                                    | UMS                  | Ongoing  |

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the structured methodology for deploying and migrating the solution for UMS, including pre-migration, pilot, production, and post-migration/support phases.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

The following table details all preparatory tasks required prior to migration, including assigned responsibilities, planned start dates, and current status.

| No | Activities                                                        | Planned Start   | Action By        | Status       | Remarks |
|----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------------|--------------|---------|
| 1  | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off          | Fri, 7-Nov-25   | Enfrasys, UMS    | Not-Started  |         |
| 2  | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25  | UMS              | Not-Started  |         |
| 3  | Fabric Environment Setup                                           | Wed, 12-Nov-25  | Enfrasys         | Not-Started  |         |
| 4  | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25  | Enfrasys, UMS    | Not-Started  |         |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot phase is designed to validate the migration approach and ensure readiness for production rollout. The pilot will be executed over a one-week period, following this timeline:

- **D-7 to D-1:**  
  - Enfrasys Solutions will configure the pilot environment, including the deployment of Microsoft Fabric, setup of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and initial data ingestion from the staging area database.
  - UMS shall validate connectivity, data integrity, and environment readiness.

- **D0 (Pilot Cutover):**  
  - Enfrasys Solutions will conduct the pilot migration of a subset of data and workloads.
  - UMS will perform user acceptance testing and provide feedback for adjustments.

## 8.3 Production

The production deployment will follow a structured process to ensure seamless migration and operational continuity.

- Enfrasys Solutions shall execute the full migration of all agreed data sources and workloads to the Microsoft Fabric environment.
- UMS shall validate the environment, perform functional testing, and confirm acceptance of the production deployment.
- Enfrasys Solutions will monitor migration progress, address issues, and provide status updates to UMS.
- Upon completion, Enfrasys Solutions will formally hand over the production environment to UMS.

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

The post-migration phase ensures operational stability and knowledge transfer to UMS.

- **D+1 (Day After Production Cutover):**
    - Enfrasys Solutions will provide hypercare support for immediate issue resolution.
    - Enfrasys Solutions will conduct a knowledge transfer session and deliver all relevant documentation to UMS.
    - UMS shall assume responsibility for ongoing environment management and support, with Enfrasys Solutions available for escalation support as needed.

# 9.0 Appendix

This section provides reference links, prerequisites, and official Microsoft documentation for each major solution area. UMS shall refer to these resources for detailed product boundaries, supported configurations, and operational guidance.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the External User to the UMS Fabric.  
- Before reaching the UMS Fabric, the request passes through the Conditional Access layer, which evaluates the user's credentials and compliance with security policies.  
- Upon successful validation, the user is granted access to the UMS Fabric resources hosted in the Fabric-RG.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, either granting or denying access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed to interact with Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various services and tools, such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Data Engineering.
     - Synapse Data Science.
     - Synapse Data Warehousing.
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics.
     - Power BI.
   - These components are unified under **OneLake**, a single data lake for storage and management.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access to its services.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI. Below is a detailed description of the components and their interactions:

1. **UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse)**:
   - Acts as the source system where raw data is stored.
   - Data is ingested from this staging area into the pipeline.

2. **Data Factory**:
   - Facilitates the ingestion of data from the UMS Staging Area into the processing pipeline.
   - Orchestrates the movement and transformation of data through the pipeline.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the first layer of data processing.
   - Stores raw or minimally processed data ingested from the staging area.
   - Acts as the foundation for further transformations.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the second layer of data processing.
   - Stores cleaned, enriched, and structured data derived from the Bronze layer.
   - Prepares data for advanced analytics and machine learning.

5. **Data Science**:
   - Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes.
   - Utilizes the Silver layer data for predictive modeling, insights generation, and other data science tasks.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the final layer of data processing.
   - Stores highly curated, business-ready data optimized for reporting and visualization.
   - Data is transformed and aggregated for end-user consumption.

7. **Identity_SM**:
   - Likely a service or component for managing identity and security within the pipeline.
   - Ensures secure access and governance of data.

8. **OneLake**:
   - Serves as a unified data storage layer.
   - Provides seamless integration and connectivity between the data pipeline and Power BI.

9. **Power BI**:
   - Acts as the visualization and reporting tool.
   - Connects to the Gold layer via OneLake to generate dashboards and reports for business users.

### Data Flow:
- Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area into the pipeline using Data Factory.
- The data flows sequentially through the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers, undergoing transformations and enrichment at each stage.
- Data Science processes are applied at the Silver layer for advanced analytics.
- The Gold layer provides business-ready data, which is securely accessed via OneLake and visualized in Power BI.

This architecture ensures a structured, secure, and scalable approach to data processing and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes data from the on-premises SQL Server via the On-Premise Data Gateway.
   - The UMS Fabric likely provides advanced data processing, analytics, or integration capabilities.

**Data Flow**:
- Data originates in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 database.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely connects to the SQL Server, retrieves the data, and transmits it to the UMS Fabric.
- The connection between the On-Premise Data Gateway and UMS Fabric is outbound and secure, eliminating the need for inbound ports, enhancing security.

This architecture supports hybrid data integration scenarios, ensuring secure and efficient data flow between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - Automates the machine learning process.
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Conducts automated experiments to generate models.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Scores the results of the AutoML experiments using a notebook.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - Allows for custom model development.
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Involves manual creation and training of models using notebooks.

4. **Processed Data: XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Receives outputs from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes.

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A system component or service that interacts with the Gold tables for further processing or identity management.

6. **Power BI**:
   - Connects to the Gold tables via **OneLake**.
   - Provides visualization and reporting capabilities for the processed data.

### Workflow Summary:
- Data from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** is processed through either **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** workflows.
- The results are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Identity_SM** interacts with the Gold tables for additional processing.
- The final data is visualized in **Power BI**, connected through **OneLake**.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides a concise summary of the UMS Power BI & Fabric project, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and its intended audience, with clear delineation of responsibilities between Enfrasys Solutions and UMS.

## 2.1 Project Overview

The UMS Power BI & Fabric project is designed to establish a modern data workflow that spans the entire lifecycle from data ingestion through transformation, advanced analytics, and visualization. The project scope includes the implementation of a Microsoft Fabric workspace, development of end-to-end ETL pipelines, execution of machine learning processes, and integration with Power BI for business-aligned analytics. Key Microsoft Fabric services to be utilized are Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. The technical objectives are to enable scalable, secure, and efficient data operations that align with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and business intelligence requirements.

**Responsibilities:**
- Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and implementation of the technical solution, including architecture, configuration, and integration of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI components.
- UMS is responsible for reviewing, providing sign-off on design decisions, and operationalizing the delivered solution post-implementation.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

The purpose of this Solution Design Document (SDD) is to formally capture and communicate the architectural blueprint, design considerations, and implementation approach for the UMS Power BI & Fabric project. The SDD serves as the authoritative reference throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring alignment between business objectives, technical requirements, and delivery outcomes. It provides UMS with a comprehensive understanding of the solution, supporting informed decision-making and effective governance.

**Responsibilities:**
- Enfrasys Solutions is accountable for authoring, updating, and maintaining the SDD.
- UMS is responsible for reviewing the document, providing feedback, and granting formal approval prior to implementation.

## 2.3 Document Audience

This Solution Design Document is intended for the following stakeholders:

- UMS IT Department
- UMS Data Analytics Team
- UMS Project Sponsors and Business Stakeholders
- Enfrasys Solutions Technical and Delivery Teams

**Responsibilities:**
- Enfrasys Solutions will ensure the SDD is accessible and comprehensible to all technical and project stakeholders.
- UMS stakeholders are expected to review the document for accuracy, completeness, and alignment with organizational objectives, and to participate in sign-off and operationalization activities.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric, Power BI & Entra ID Overview

This section provides an overview of the core Microsoft technologies—Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID—that form the foundation of the UMS data platform solution.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. Fabric consolidates multiple data services into a single SaaS platform, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, store, analyze, and visualize data at scale.

Core services within Microsoft Fabric include:

- **Data Factory**: A data integration and orchestration service that enables ingestion from diverse sources, transformation, and movement of data across the platform.
- **Synapse**: A comprehensive analytics service for big data processing, advanced analytics, and data warehousing.
- **Data Science**: A collaborative environment for building, training, and deploying machine learning models.
- **OneLake**: A unified, secure data lake for storing and managing all organizational data in a single location.

UMS shall leverage Microsoft Fabric to establish a robust data workflow, from ingestion through transformation to advanced analytics, supporting business-aligned decision making.

### Microsoft Fabric Summary Table

| Technology      | Description                                                                                                      | Role in Solution                                                    |
|-----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture and connectivity design for the UMS Fabric deployment, focusing on secure on-premises to cloud integration and the implementation of the On-Premises Data Gateway.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Microsoft Fabric is a unified SaaS analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within the Microsoft cloud ecosystem. It provides a seamless experience for ingesting, transforming, and visualizing data, leveraging secure connectivity between on-premises and cloud environments.

UMS shall implement a hybrid network architecture that enables secure connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric resources hosted in Azure. The design leverages outbound-only connections for all hybrid integrations, ensuring that no inbound ports are opened on the on-premises network perimeter. Private Link and similar private endpoint solutions are not implemented in this phase, which imposes certain limitations on private connectivity and may expose metadata to the public internet (over encrypted channels).

Key features of the network design include:
- Outbound HTTPS connectivity from on-premises to Azure.
- Use of Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) to enforce granular outbound rules.
- Secure authentication via Microsoft Entra ID.
- No inbound access to on-premises resources from the cloud.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbacfce7-ce3f-40db-a1f9-60bef2858356\images\slide_006_img_002.png)
Figure 1: Conditional Access and secure inbound authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric.

## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway

The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a Microsoft component that facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources (such as SQL Server) and Microsoft cloud services including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Data Factory. The gateway acts as a bridge, relaying data via outbound encrypted connections without requiring any inbound firewall rules.

### 4.2.1 OPDG Role and Placement

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that the OPDG be deployed within a dedicated virtual machine (VM) located in the UMS on-premises environment. This VM will host the gateway agent, manage secure outbound connections to Microsoft endpoints, and ensure compliance with organizational security policies.

- Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design, configuration, and deployment of the OPDG VM and agent.
- UMS shall provision the necessary on-premises infrastructure prerequisites, including network access, VM hosting, and required permissions.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbacfce7-ce3f-40db-a1f9-60bef2858356\images\slide_011_img_004.png)
Figure 2: OPDG architecture bridging on-premises SQL Server and UMS Fabric.

### 4.2.2 OPDG VM Specification

The following table defines the minimum specifications for the OPDG VM to ensure optimal performance and compatibility:

| Component         | Specification                                      |
|-------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System  | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) |
| .NET Framework    | Version 4.8                                       |
| vCPU              | 8 cores                                           |
| RAM               | Minimum 8 GB                                      |

## 4.3 Outbound Security Rules (NSG)

To ensure secure and reliable connectivity, Enfrasys Solutions prescribes the following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) outbound rules for the OPDG VM. These rules restrict traffic to only the necessary Microsoft service endpoints, enforcing best practices for network security.

| Name                        | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                          | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                      | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                       | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

## 4.4 Network Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                                                 | Decision                                                                                   |
|-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | On-premises to cloud connectivity is outbound-only, no inbound firewall rules.   | Approved. Ensures minimal attack surface and compliance with UMS security policy.          |
| 2   | Private Link and private endpoints are not implemented in this phase.            | Approved. Simpler deployment but limits private metadata transfer.                         |
| 3   | OPDG is deployed on a dedicated VM in the on-premises environment.               | Approved. Ensures separation of duties and resource isolation.                             |
| 4   | NSG rules restrict outbound traffic to specific Microsoft endpoints.             | Approved. Enforces least privilege and network segmentation.                               |

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decisions.

The following table summarizes the key design considerations for the UMS data workflow, encompassing ingestion, transformation, machine learning, and reporting:

| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                        | Data Factory    |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                       | Data Engineering|
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                            | Data Engineering|
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                           | Data Engineering|
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering|
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory    |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science    |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                | Power BI        |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                     | Power BI        |

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security design for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric deployment, focusing on outbound network security, encryption, and access control.

## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that enables administrators to control inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources based on rules. NSGs are applied at the subnet or network interface level and enforce granular access policies.

For UMS, only outbound rules are defined to support secure communication between the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM and Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services.

### Outbound NSG Rules

| Name                        | Priority | Source                  | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net                                   | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                           | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement all NSG rules. UMS shall review and approve the final rule set prior to deployment.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a mandatory control for all UMS data flows, ensuring confidentiality and integrity both at rest and in transit.

### Encryption at Rest

- **Microsoft Fabric**: Microsoft Fabric encrypts all customer data at rest using Microsoft-managed keys following Azure Storage encryption standards (AES-256).
- **Power BI**: Power BI datasets and artifacts are encrypted at rest in the service using Microsoft-managed keys.
- **OneLake**: OneLake, as part of Microsoft Fabric, provides encryption at rest for all stored data using platform-managed keys.

### Encryption in Transit

All data flows between UMS resources and Microsoft cloud services are encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.

- **SQL Server to Fabric**: Data transferred from on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via the On-Premises Data Gateway is secured using TLS.
- **Fabric to OneLake**: Internal data movement within Fabric, including to OneLake, is encrypted using Microsoft’s secure backbone and TLS.
- **Power BI Access**: User access to Power BI services and datasets is always encrypted in transit via HTTPS.

Enfrasys Solutions shall define and implement all encryption controls. UMS shall enforce compliance with user security policies, including the use of supported clients and secure protocols.

## 7.3 Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication

### Microsoft Entra ID Overview

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication, authorization, and identity governance for Microsoft 365, Azure, and thousands of other SaaS applications.

### Conditional Access and MFA

Due to current licensing constraints, UMS is not eligible for advanced Conditional Access policies (P1/P2). As a compensating control, Enfrasys Solutions has enforced Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level for all UMS users accessing Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related services.

#### Conditional Access Design Decisions

| No.  | Design Decision                                                                                      | Decision                        |
|------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred (pending license)      |
| DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy                                                    | Deferred (pending license)      |
| DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts                | Enforced (tenant-level)         |
| DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment                                                  | Enabled via MFA                 |

Enfrasys Solutions shall configure and monitor MFA enforcement. UMS shall ensure all users complete MFA registration and comply with authentication prompts.

### 8.3.2 Production Deployment Tasks

| Item | Activities                                                        | Action By      | Status       |
|------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|--------------|
| 1    | Confirm production environment configuration                      | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 2    | Migrate data pipelines and reports from pilot to production       | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 3    | UMS to provide access credentials and final validation            | UMS           | Not-Started  |
| 4    | Cutover to production data ingestion                             | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 5    | User acceptance testing and sign-off                             | UMS           | Not-Started  |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Following migration, Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support, knowledge transfer, and formal handover to UMS. UMS is responsible for sign-off and ongoing operational management.

| Item | Activities                                           | Action By      | Status       |
|------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------|--------------|
| 1    | Monitor system stability and address post-migration issues | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 2    | Conduct knowledge transfer and administrator training | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 3    | Handover documentation and operational guides         | Enfrasys      | Not-Started  |
| 4    | UMS to confirm acceptance and sign-off                | UMS           | Not-Started  |

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving the following components and data flow:

1. **Fabric-RG**:  
   - Represents a resource group in Azure, likely hosting resources related to the "UMS Fabric."  
   - The resource group acts as a logical container for managing and organizing Azure resources.

2. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.  
   - It appears to be the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access**:  
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.  
   - It enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, location, or risk level.

4. **External User**:  
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.  
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to ensure security.

**Data Flow**:  
- The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of interaction or access from the External User to the UMS Fabric.  
- Before reaching the UMS Fabric, the request passes through the Conditional Access layer, which evaluates the user's credentials and compliance with security policies.  
- Upon successful validation, the user gains access to the UMS Fabric resources hosted within the Fabric-RG.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed to interact with Microsoft Fabric.  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse.
     - Power BI.
     - OneLake (a unified data lake for storage).

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the Azure ecosystem, leveraging UMS Fabric, Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI. Below is a detailed description of the components and their interactions:

1. **UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse)**:
   - Acts as the source system where raw data resides.
   - Data is ingested from this staging area into the pipeline.

2. **Data Factory**:
   - Facilitates the ingestion of data from the UMS Staging Area into the processing pipeline.
   - Orchestrates the movement and transformation of data through the pipeline.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the first layer of data processing.
   - Stores raw or minimally processed data ingested from the staging area.
   - Acts as the foundation for further transformations.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the second layer of data processing.
   - Contains cleaned, enriched, and structured data derived from the Bronze layer.
   - Prepares data for advanced analytics and machine learning.

5. **Data Science**:
   - Involves advanced analytics and machine learning processes.
   - Utilizes data from the Silver layer for predictive modeling, insights generation, and other data science tasks.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the final layer of data processing.
   - Stores highly curated, business-ready data optimized for reporting and visualization.
   - Data is prepared for consumption by downstream systems.

7. **Identity_SM**:
   - Likely a service or component for managing identity and security within the pipeline.
   - Ensures secure access and governance of data.

8. **OneLake**:
   - Serves as a unified data lake for storing and managing data across the pipeline.
   - Provides seamless integration with other Azure services.

9. **Power BI**:
   - Connects to the Gold layer via OneLake.
   - Enables data visualization, reporting, and business intelligence for end-users.

### Data Flow:
- Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area into the pipeline using Data Factory.
- The data flows sequentially through the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers, undergoing transformations and enrichment at each stage.
- Data Science processes are applied at the Silver layer to derive insights.
- The final curated data in the Gold layer is made available for reporting and visualization in Power BI via OneLake.

This architecture ensures a structured, secure, and scalable approach to data processing and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data through the OPDG.
   - Likely used for advanced analytics, reporting, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows bidirectionally between the SQL Server database and the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway establishes a secure outbound connection to the UMS Fabric, enabling data transfer without exposing the on-premises environment to inbound traffic.
- This architecture ensures secure, seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - Automates the machine learning process.
     - Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an **AutoML Experiment**.
     - The experiment generates a model, and the results are scored using a notebook.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - Allows for custom model development.
     - Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is used for **Modeling** via a notebook.

4. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where processed and scored results are stored.
   - Acts as the output of both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes.

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A component for identity or metadata management, integrated with the Gold tables.

6. **Power BI**:
   - Used for data visualization and reporting.
   - Connects to XX_Gold_LK Tables via **OneLake** for real-time insights.

### Summary:
The workflow begins with data in the Silver layer (XX_Silver_LK Tables) stored in OneLake. This data is processed through either AutoML or Manual Machine Learning, with results stored in the Gold layer (XX_Gold_LK Tables). The Gold layer is then connected to Power BI for visualization, enabling business intelligence and decision-making. Identity_SM ensures proper metadata or identity management within the system.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
