# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the project scope, objectives, and the intent of this Solution Design Document for UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation. ## 2.1 Project Overview UMS is undertaking a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics landscape through the adoption of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The project encompasses the design and implementation of a comprehensive data workflow, spanning data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and visualization. The scope includes the establishment of a dedicated Fabric workspace, the development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, execution of machine learning processes, and seamless integration with Power BI for business-aligned analytics. This initiative is aligned with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy and leverages Microsoft Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Fabric Data Science for machine learning applications. ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the design, deployment, and governance of UMS’s Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. It provides detailed technical guidance and decision rationale to ensure a secure, scalable, and business-aligned implementation. The document is intended to guide both Enfrasys Solutions and UMS throughout all project phases, supporting effective collaboration, risk mitigation, and compliance with best practices. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this Solution Design Document includes: - UMS IT Department - UMS Business Stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions Architects and Consultants - UMS Project Sponsors - UMS Data Governance Team Responsibility for review and formal sign-off of this document is jointly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides formal definitions and context for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID as foundational components in the UMS analytics solution. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform delivered as a Software as a Service (SaaS) solution by Microsoft. It unifies multiple data and analytics services into a single, cohesive environment, enabling organizations to manage data ingestion, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a unified workspace. Microsoft Fabric’s core services include: - **Data Factory**: This service provides cloud-scale data integration and orchestration, enabling the ingestion, transformation, and movement of data from diverse sources into a central repository for analytics. - **Synapse**: Fabric Synapse delivers advanced analytics capabilities, including big data processing, data warehousing, and real-time analytics, supporting both structured and unstructured data workloads. - **Data Science**: This component offers machine learning and advanced analytics tools, empowering data scientists to build, train, and deploy predictive models directly within the Fabric environment. Microsoft Fabric plays a critical role in modern analytics by providing a seamless, end-to-end platform for data engineering, advanced analytics, and machine learning, all governed by enterprise-grade security and compliance. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and drive informed decision-making across an organization. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing data prepared and processed within Fabric to be seamlessly consumed for reporting and dashboarding. Key capabilities of Power BI include: - Interactive data visualization and exploration - Self-service and enterprise-scale business intelligence - Integration with a wide range of data sources, including those managed within Microsoft Fabric - Collaboration and sharing features for secure distribution of insights Power BI’s integration with Fabric ensures that data pipelines, machine learning outputs, and analytics results are easily accessible for business-aligned analytics, supporting UMS’s objectives for end-to-end data-driven decision-making. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and access control for users, devices, and applications across Microsoft cloud services and integrated third-party solutions. In the context of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, Microsoft Entra ID is responsible for: - Centralized identity management for all users accessing analytics resources - Enforcing Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant access to data and services - Enabling role-based access control (RBAC) for granular permission management within Fabric workspaces and Power BI assets Microsoft Entra ID ensures that only authorized UMS personnel can access sensitive analytics assets, supporting compliance and security requirements throughout the data workflow. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section defines the network architecture for UMS, focusing on secure hybrid connectivity between on-premises SQL Server, the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG), and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview UMS’s network topology is designed to enable secure, reliable data integration between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The architecture leverages an On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) deployed on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s network. The OPDG acts as a secure bridge, facilitating outbound-only connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, thereby eliminating the need to open inbound firewall ports. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure hybrid data flow between on-premises SQL Server, OPDG, and UMS Fabric. Key components: - **On-Premises SQL Server 2017**: Hosts UMS’s data warehouse and staging area. - **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)**: Deployed on a dedicated VM, it establishes outbound HTTPS connections to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure services. - **UMS Fabric (Cloud)**: Receives and processes data via the secure gateway. All network security group (NSG) rules supporting this architecture are defined in Section 7.0. Only outbound connectivity is permitted from the OPDG VM to Microsoft endpoints, ensuring minimal attack surface. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric: Formal Definition Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) by Microsoft. It unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single platform, leveraging OneLake as a unified data lake. Fabric enables organizations to ingest, transform, and analyze data securely and at scale. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG): Role and Deployment The On-Premises Data Gateway is a Microsoft component that securely facilitates data transfer between on-premises systems and Microsoft cloud services, such as Fabric and Power BI. The gateway agent is installed on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s data center. It authenticates and transmits data using outbound-only HTTPS connections, ensuring compliance with security best practices. #### Deployment Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------------------- |-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Provision VM according to specification; OS hardening; network configuration | UMS | Pending | | .NET Framework Installation | Install .NET Framework 4.8 on the OPDG VM | UMS | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Deploy and configure Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway agent on the VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Outbound Firewall Rule Configuration | Ensure outbound access to required Microsoft endpoints (per Section 7.0 NSG rules) | UMS | Pending | | Gateway Registration with Fabric | Register and validate the gateway with Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | #### OPDG VM Specifications | Component | Specification | |-------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | #### Outbound Connectivity Summary The OPDG VM requires only outbound HTTPS (TCP 443) and HTTP (TCP 80 for connectivity tests) to Microsoft cloud endpoints. No inbound ports are opened, and all traffic is governed by explicit NSG rules (see Section 7.0). #### Diagram Reference ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 2: OPDG deployment bridging on-premises SQL Server and UMS Fabric. All network security group (NSG) rules for the OPDG VM are formally defined in Section 7.0 and must be strictly enforced by UMS. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignment processes for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within the UMS environment. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. The Microsoft Fabric Administrator role is a privileged role responsible for managing platform-level settings, governance, and security across all Fabric workloads. **Responsibilities of the Fabric Admin include:** - Managing tenant-level settings and configurations. - Assigning and revoking workspace roles. - Overseeing security, compliance, and audit activities. - Monitoring usage and performance. - Coordinating with Microsoft support for incident resolution. **Assignment Process:** Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the initial assignment of the Fabric Admin role to designated UMS personnel as per the agreed role mapping. UMS shall be responsible for the ongoing management, periodic review, and adjustment of Fabric Admin assignments to ensure alignment with organizational requirements and compliance mandates. ## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide several native roles, each with specific permissions and responsibilities. The following table summarizes the key workspace-level roles and their capabilities: | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |-----------------------------|:-----:|:-----------:|:------:|:------:| | Manage workspace settings | ✓ | | | | | Add/remove users | ✓ | | | | | Publish datasets/reports | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | | Edit content | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | | View content | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | Share content | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | Delete workspace | ✓ | | | | **Role Definitions:** - **Admin:** Full control over workspace, including user management and workspace deletion. - **Contributor:** Can publish, edit, and share content but cannot manage users or delete workspace. - **Member:** Can publish and edit content, but with limited administrative rights. - **Viewer:** Read-only access to workspace content. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS assign roles based on the principle of least privilege, ensuring users have only the permissions necessary for their responsibilities. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access The following table presents the workspace role mapping, suggested roles, and designated UMS personnel-in-charge (PIC): | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|----------------------|----------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi | **Role Assignment and Management:** - **Initial Role Mapping:** Enfrasys Solutions shall execute the initial mapping and assignment of workspace roles to UMS personnel as per the above table. - **Ongoing Role Management:** UMS shall be responsible for maintaining, reviewing, and updating workspace role assignments to ensure operational integrity and compliance. This structured approach ensures that UMS personnel have appropriate access levels to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources, supporting both security and productivity objectives. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section defines the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI architecture, workflow, and governance as recommended for UMS. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations **Microsoft Fabric** is an end-to-end analytics platform from Microsoft, integrating data movement, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a unified SaaS experience. Fabric leverages OneLake, Data Factory, and Power BI as core components. **Power BI** is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform, enabling data modeling, visualization, and sharing of insights across organizations. The following table summarizes the design considerations for UMS’s data workflow from ingestion to visualization: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions The following table documents the rationale for each design decision in the UMS workflow: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion from UMS Staging Area (SQL Server 2017) will utilize Azure Data Factory pipelines. | Ensures scalable and automated data movement into Fabric. | | 2 | Implementation of Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouse layers. | Follows best practices for data lakehouse architecture, supporting raw, cleansed, and curated data. | | 3 | Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query will be used for transformation and cleansing. | Provides flexibility for both code-based and visual data engineering. | | 4 | AutoML and Manual ML workflows will be supported in the Data Science layer. | Enables UMS to leverage both automated and custom machine learning approaches as per project needs. | | 5 | Model outputs and scoring results will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Ensures a single source of truth for analytics and reporting. | | 6 | Semantic Models will be built on top of the Gold Lakehouse and exposed to Power BI via OneLake. | Delivers high-performance analytics and consistent data consumption for business users. | | 7 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model for dashboard and report creation. | Empowers analysts and business users with self-service BI capabilities. | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and Manual ML workflow integration in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions Access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI must be governed by robust identity and access management policies. The following sub-sections outline the key methods and decisions. ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Conditional Access policies in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) provide adaptive access control based on user, location, device, and risk. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Require compliant device and trusted location for administrative access to Fabric/Power BI| Enforced for all admins and data engineers. | | 2 | Block legacy authentication protocols | Enforced to reduce attack surface. | ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) MFA is a mandatory security control for accessing sensitive data and administrative functions. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Enforce MFA for all users with data engineering or admin roles | Required for all privileged accounts. | | 2 | Optional MFA for read-only/report consumers | Recommended but not enforced for report viewers. | ### 6.3.3 Direct User Access Direct user access to data sources (e.g., SQL Server) via Data Gateway or to Power BI workspaces must be tightly controlled. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|--------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Assign least-privilege roles in Power BI workspaces | Only necessary permissions are granted per user function. | | 2 | Restrict Data Gateway access to approved service accounts | Only Data Factory and Fabric services permitted. | ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) will be deployed on a dedicated VM to bridge on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. | Component | Specification | |------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 3: Data Gateway VM bridging on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS organize all Fabric and Power BI resources under a dedicated management group within the UMS Azure tenant. This facilitates centralized policy enforcement, cost tracking, and delegated administration. All resource groups for data, compute, and analytics workloads shall be assigned to this management group. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging Consistent naming and tagging are essential for resource governance, automation, and cost management. The following table defines the naming convention for key Fabric items: | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | Acronyms used: - LK: Lakehouse - DF: Dataflow - SM: Semantic Model - PL: Pipeline - WS: Workspace ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines UMS shall implement the following governance disciplines for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI: | Resource | Cost Center | Owner | Threshold | |------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------| | Fabric Capacity | Data Analytics | UMS IT | RM 10,000 | | Workspaces | Student Affairs | UMS BI Lead | RM 2,000 | | Data Gateway VM | Infrastructure | UMS Infra | RM 1,000 | Cost management tools such as Azure Cost Management + Billing shall be configured for automated alerts and reporting. ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions The following table defines triggers, actions, and ownership for governance violations: | Trigger | Action | Owner | |-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | Resource cost exceeds threshold | Automated alert and review by cost center owner | UMS | | Unauthorized access attempt | Security incident raised, access reviewed | UMS | | Naming convention violation | Resource flagged for remediation | UMS | | Policy non-compliance (e.g., MFA) | Access revoked until compliance restored | UMS | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial governance design and deployment. UMS is responsible for ongoing operational governance and compliance monitoring. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section details the security controls, network security group (NSG) rules, and encryption mechanisms for the UMS Microsoft Fabric and Power BI deployment. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that allows organizations to enforce inbound and outbound network traffic rules at the subnet and network interface level. NSGs help protect Azure resources by restricting traffic to only what is necessary for business operations. Enfrasys Solutions recommends and will implement the following outbound NSG rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine to ensure secure connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring and periodic review of NSG effectiveness. ### Outbound NSG Rules for Data Gateway VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the above NSG rules during deployment. UMS shall monitor and review the NSG rules to ensure continued compliance with organizational security policies. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a fundamental security control for protecting data confidentiality and integrity, both at rest and in transit. The following encryption mechanisms are recommended and will be implemented for the UMS Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Entra ID integration. ### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest **Definition:** Encryption at rest refers to the cryptographic protection of data stored on disk or persistent storage, ensuring that data cannot be accessed in plaintext by unauthorized users or processes. **Microsoft Fabric & Power BI:** All data stored in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default. This includes data stored in OneLake, data warehouses, and Power BI datasets. Encryption is performed using industry-standard AES-256 algorithms. **On-Premises Data Gateway:** Any temporary data or logs stored on the Data Gateway VM must be protected using BitLocker or an equivalent disk encryption solution. **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Ensure Azure resources and storage accounts are configured to use encryption at rest. - UMS: Ensure compliance and periodic validation of encryption status. ### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit **Definition:** Encryption in transit ensures that data moving between clients, on-premises infrastructure, and cloud services is protected from interception or tampering using secure transport protocols. **On-Premises to Cloud (OPDG to Microsoft Fabric/Power BI):** All data transmitted between the On-Premises Data Gateway and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI services is protected using HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or above). This includes authentication traffic to Entra ID and all data transfer operations. **Within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI:** Internal service communications within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are secured using TLS encryption, ensuring data remains protected as it moves between components (e.g., from OneLake to Power BI). **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions: Configure all endpoints and gateways to enforce HTTPS/TLS for all communications. - UMS: Ensure users and client applications access services only via secure (HTTPS) endpoints. ### 7.2.3 Encryption Mechanism Summary | No. | Security Mechanism | Description | Responsibility | |-----|---------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------| | 1 | Encryption at Rest | AES-256 for all data in Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure storage accounts | Enfrasys, UMS | | 2 | Encryption in Transit | TLS 1.2+ for all data flows between on-premises, cloud, and internal service communications | Enfrasys, UMS | | 3 | Disk Encryption (Data Gateway VM) | BitLocker or equivalent for VM disks | Enfrasys, UMS | ## 7.3 Conditional Access and MFA Enforcement **Microsoft Entra Conditional Access** is a policy-based security feature that evaluates user and device signals to enforce access controls for Microsoft cloud services. For UMS, Conditional Access is not currently eligible due to licensing constraints; therefore, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level to provide an additional security layer. | No. | Conditional Access Policy Decision | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, SQL DB, Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enfrasys Solutions will provide guidance on Conditional Access policy configuration if UMS upgrades licensing in the future. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access architecture for UMS Fabric external access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\9470500b-93b4-4995-af47-b70e65e8597d\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Secure authentication flow with Entra Conditional Access policies. --- Enfrasys Solutions shall implement all NSG and encryption configurations as specified. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing compliance monitoring, user education, and periodic review of security controls. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the phased deployment and migration approach for UMS, including preparatory, pilot, production, and post-migration/support activities. Each phase is structured with clear timelines, responsibilities, and sign-off checkpoints. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup (D-7) The Pre-Migration/Setup phase encompasses all preparatory activities required to ensure a successful deployment. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technical setup, while UMS is accountable for business-side validation and confirmations. All preparatory activities must be completed and signed off by D-7. **Sign-off Checkpoint:** UMS and Enfrasys must formally sign off on the completion of all pre-migration activities before proceeding to the pilot phase. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway| Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys / UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot (D-3) The Pilot phase validates the migration approach with a limited dataset and user group. Enfrasys Solutions will execute the technical pilot migration, while UMS will conduct business validation and provide feedback. The pilot must be completed and signed off by D-3. **Pilot Scope:** - Migrate a representative subset of data from the staging area to Microsoft Fabric. - Test data connectivity and integrity via the On-Premises Data Gateway. - Validate core reporting and analytics use cases. **Pilot Activities and Responsibilities:** | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------------| | 1 | Execute pilot migration of sample dataset to Fabric | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Validate data accuracy and accessibility in Fabric | UMS | Pending | | 3 | Collect pilot feedback and document issues | UMS / Enfrasys | Pending | | 4 | Remediate pilot findings and obtain UMS sign-off | Enfrasys / UMS | Pending | **Sign-off Checkpoint:** UMS must provide formal acceptance of pilot migration results prior to production cutover. ## 8.3 Production (D0) The Production phase entails the full migration of data and activation of the Microsoft Fabric environment for all intended workloads. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technical execution, while UMS validates business operations post-migration. All production steps are executed on D0. **Production Deployment Steps:** | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------------| | 1 | Execute full data migration from staging area to Fabric | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Switch data sources for production reporting to Fabric | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | Conduct smoke testing of production workloads | Enfrasys | Pending | | 4 | Validate business operations and reporting in production | UMS | Pending | | 5 | Obtain UMS sign-off for production cutover | UMS | Pending | **Sign-off Checkpoint:** UMS must confirm successful business validation and sign off on production migration completion. ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support (D+1) The Post-Migration/Support phase provides stabilization, knowledge transfer, and support to ensure a seamless transition. Enfrasys Solutions delivers technical support and documentation, while UMS assumes operational responsibility after handover. This phase commences on D+1. **Post-Migration/Support Activities:** | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------------| | 1 | Provide post-migration technical support (hypercare) | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Conduct knowledge transfer and handover sessions | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | UMS assumes operational responsibility | UMS | Pending | | 4 | Final project closure and sign-off | UMS / Enfrasys | Pending | **Sign-off Checkpoint:** UMS and Enfrasys jointly sign off on project closure after successful handover and support completion. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference material, technical prerequisites, and official Microsoft documentation links for each major solution area. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Azure Virtual Machines documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Title: Azure Compute Overview [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/topics/compute](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/topics/compute) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Azure subscription and service limits, quotas, and constraints [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Title: Sizes for virtual machines in Azure [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) ### Others Title: Azure VM best practices [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/best-practices) Title: Azure Managed Disks documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/managed-disks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/managed-disks-overview) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Azure Virtual Network documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) Title: Azure Networking Overview [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Azure networking limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-service-limits) Title: NSG limits and supported configurations [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Others Title: Azure ExpressRoute documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/) Title: Azure Application Gateway documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Microsoft Entra ID documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) Title: Azure Security documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Entra ID service limits and restrictions [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-users/directory-service-limits-restrictions) Title: Azure Security Center quotas and limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-quotas) ### Others Title: Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/) Title: Azure Key Vault documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Azure Monitor documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Title: Azure Log Analytics documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Azure Monitor service limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitor-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitor-service-limits) Title: Log Analytics workspace limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) ### Others Title: Azure Monitor best practices [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices) Title: Monitoring Azure resources [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitor-azure-resources](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/monitor-azure-resources) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Azure Governance documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Title: Azure Policy documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Azure Policy and Initiative limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/definition-structure#limits) Title: Management group and subscription limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/management-groups#limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/management-groups#limits) ### Others Title: Azure Blueprints documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/blueprints/) Title: Cloud Adoption Framework [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Microsoft Fabric documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/) Title: Power BI documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Microsoft Fabric capacity and limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/limits) Title: Power BI service limits [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacity](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-limits-quotas-capacity) ### Others Title: Power BI security whitepaper [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/security-whitepaper](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/security-whitepaper) Title: Microsoft Fabric best practices [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/fabric-best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/get-started/fabric-best-practices)

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric component, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents a logical container in Azure that holds related resources for the UMS Fabric.
   - The Resource Group is depicted as the boundary for managing and organizing the UMS Fabric resources.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely represents a unified management system or platform that provides specific functionalities or services.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.
   - Enforces policies based on conditions such as user identity, device compliance, or location.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric from outside the organization.
   - Access is mediated through Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant interactions.

5. **Data Flow:**
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric to the External User.
   - Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, ensuring that only authorized users can access the UMS Fabric.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users interacting with the UMS Fabric, leveraging Azure's Conditional Access policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

1. **User Authentication**:
   - The process begins with a **user** attempting to authenticate to **Microsoft Fabric**.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:
   - The authentication request is evaluated against **Entra conditional access policies**. This step involves:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - **Users and groups**: Identifying the user or group attempting access.
       - **Network location**: Determining the geographical or network context of the request.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to **Microsoft Fabric**, which includes various components:
     - **Microsoft Fabric Services**: Represented by icons for services such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence.
     - **OneLake**: A unified data lake for storing and managing data within Microsoft Fabric.

This diagram illustrates the secure authentication flow and conditional access enforcement for users accessing Microsoft Fabric services.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the **Microsoft Fabric** ecosystem, leveraging **Azure Data Factory**, **OneLake**, and **Power BI** for data ingestion, transformation, storage, and visualization.

---

### Components and Data Flow:

1. **Data Source:**
   - **UMS Staging Area**: A Microsoft SQL Server 2017-based data warehouse serves as the initial data source.
   - Data is ingested from this staging area into the pipeline.

2. **Data Ingestion:**
   - **Data Factory**: Azure Data Factory is used to orchestrate and automate the ingestion of data from the staging area into the processing pipeline.

3. **Data Lakehouse Layers:**
   - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: Represents the raw data layer where ingested data is stored in its original format.
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the cleaned and enriched data layer, where transformations and validations are applied to the raw data.
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the curated and aggregated data layer, optimized for analytics and reporting.

4. **Data Transformation and Science:**
   - **Data Factory**: Used again for orchestrating data transformations between the Silver and Gold layers.
   - **Data Science**: Advanced analytics and machine learning processes are applied to the data in the Silver layer to derive insights and predictions.

5. **Identity Management:**
   - **Identity_SM**: A component for managing identity and security, ensuring secure access to the Gold layer data.

6. **Data Storage:**
   - **OneLake**: A unified data lake storage solution that integrates with the Fabric ecosystem, providing a single source of truth for all data layers.

7. **Data Visualization:**
   - **Power BI**: Connected to the Gold layer via OneLake, enabling the creation of interactive dashboards and reports for business intelligence.

---

### Summary:
This architecture demonstrates a modern data lakehouse approach using Microsoft Fabric, with Azure Data Factory for orchestration, OneLake for unified storage, and Power BI for visualization. It ensures a seamless flow from raw data ingestion to actionable insights.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data locally before it is transferred or consumed by other systems.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes or processes the data from the on-premises SQL Server.
   - The connection to the UMS Fabric is established through the On-Premise Data Gateway.

**Data Flow**:
- Data originates in the on-premises SQL Server 2017 database.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely connects to the SQL Server and facilitates data transfer to the UMS Fabric.
- The connection is outbound-only, ensuring no inbound ports are required, enhancing security.

This architecture is designed for hybrid scenarios where on-premises data needs to be securely integrated with cloud-based services.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:
The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within a cloud-based architecture, leveraging components such as UMS Fabric, OneLake, AutoML, and Power BI for data analysis and visualization.

1. **Data Source:**
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer stored in OneLake. This serves as the input for machine learning processes.

2. **Machine Learning Pathways:**
   - **AutoML Pathway**:
     - Data from the XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into the **AutoML Experiment** module.
     - The AutoML module automates the machine learning process, including model selection and hyperparameter tuning.
     - The results are scored using a notebook, and the output is directed to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables** for further use.
   - **Manual Machine Learning Pathway**:
     - Alternatively, data from the XX_Silver_LK Tables can be processed through a **Manual Machine Learning** workflow.
     - This involves custom modeling using a notebook, allowing for more control over the machine learning process.
     - The output is also directed to the **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.

3. **Data Storage and Identity Management:**
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined and processed data layer stored in OneLake.
   - **Identity_SM**: A component for managing identity and security, ensuring secure access to the processed data.

4. **Visualization and Reporting:**
   - The processed data in the XX_Gold_LK Tables is connected to **Power BI** via OneLake for visualization and reporting.

5. **Integration:**
   - The entire workflow is encapsulated within the **UMS Fabric**, which provides a unified framework for data processing, machine learning, and analytics.

This architecture supports both automated and manual machine learning workflows, ensuring flexibility and scalability for data-driven decision-making.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric solution design, including project scope, document purpose, and intended audience. ## 2.1 Project Overview The Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) Power BI & Fabric project is scoped to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow, covering the entire lifecycle from data ingestion to transformation and visualization within Microsoft Fabric. The business objectives include: - Establishing a dedicated Microsoft Fabric workspace for UMS. - Developing an end-to-end ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline to support robust data integration and orchestration. - Enabling advanced analytics through Machine Learning processes. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned reporting and analytics. - Aligning UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s core services, including Fabric Data Factory (for data integration and orchestration), Fabric Synapse (for advanced analytics and big data processing), and Data Science (for machine learning applications). ## 2.2 Document Purpose This Solution Design Document is produced to formally define the technical and architectural approach for the Power BI & Fabric implementation at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). It serves as the authoritative reference for all solution components, design decisions, and implementation guidelines. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document; UMS shall review and provide sign-off. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: | Stakeholder Group | Role/Responsibility | |-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------| | UMS Executive Leadership | Project sponsorship, strategic alignment | | UMS IT Management | Oversight, solution governance | | UMS Data/Analytics Teams | Solution adoption, operationalization | | UMS Business Units | Business requirements, user acceptance | | Enfrasys Solutions Project Team | Solution design, implementation, and support | | Microsoft Account Team | Platform guidance, technical escalation (as required) | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for preparing and updating this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and approving the document content. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview This section provides an authoritative overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID as foundational technologies for Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) data workflow solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for technology selection and solution overview. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) by Microsoft. It unifies various data and analytics workloads—including data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence—within a single, cohesive environment. Microsoft Fabric is built around a suite of core services: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|--------------| | 1 | Data Factory: Provides data integration, orchestration, and ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) capabilities. | Data Integration | | 2 | Synapse: Offers advanced analytics, big data processing, and data warehousing. | Data Analytics | | 3 | Data Science: Enables machine learning model development, training, and deployment. | Machine Learning | | 4 | OneLake: A unified, scalable data lake that serves as a central repository for all data assets. | Data Storage | | 5 | Real-Time Analytics: Supports streaming and real-time data processing. | Real-Time Analytics | | 6 | Power BI: Embedded for business intelligence and visualization. | Business Intelligence | Microsoft Fabric’s role in the UMS solution is to orchestrate the end-to-end data workflow—from data ingestion (via Data Factory), transformation (using Synapse and Data Science), to secure storage (OneLake), and finally, to business-aligned analytics (via Power BI). This unified approach accelerates time-to-value, simplifies management, and enhances data governance for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence (BI) platform that enables organizations to visualize data, generate interactive reports, and derive actionable insights. Power BI seamlessly integrates with Microsoft Fabric, leveraging OneLake as a unified data source and supporting direct connectivity to curated data layers (Bronze, Silver, Gold). This integration ensures that Universiti Malaysia Sabah can deliver secure, scalable, and real-time analytics to business users. The business value of Power BI for UMS includes: - Democratization of data analytics for non-technical users. - Rapid development and sharing of dashboards and reports. - Built-in governance and security aligned with Microsoft Entra ID. - Native support for advanced analytics and AI-driven insights. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning workflow and Power BI integration in Microsoft Fabric. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on (SSO), and conditional access policies for users and applications. In the UMS solution, Microsoft Entra ID is the authoritative identity provider, ensuring secure access to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and all integrated services. This guarantees compliance, centralized user management, and robust security for Universiti Malaysia Sabah. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the selection, design, and overview of these technologies to ensure alignment with UMS’s business and technical requirements. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture, connectivity, and security controls for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) on-premises data sources with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI cloud services. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s solution architecture establishes secure, high-performance connectivity between on-premises SQL Server resources, a dedicated Data Gateway Virtual Machine (VM), and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI services in the cloud. The design ensures that data flows securely from the UMS data warehouse to cloud analytics platforms while adhering to best practices for network segmentation and outbound security. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: On-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric connectivity via Data Gateway VM. ### Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |----|-------------|--------------| | NC-01 | Data must flow securely from on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric/Power BI | Data Integration | | NC-02 | No inbound ports are to be opened on UMS’s network perimeter | Security | | NC-03 | Outbound connectivity must be restricted to Microsoft endpoints only | Security | | NC-04 | Data Gateway VM must be highly available and sized for peak throughput | Reliability | | NC-05 | Conditional Access policies must protect cloud access | Security | ### Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------|----------| | 1 | All data integration between UMS SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI shall be mediated by a dedicated On-Premises Data Gateway VM | Enforced | | 2 | Only outbound HTTPS (TCP 443) and required HTTP (TCP 80) traffic to Microsoft endpoints will be permitted from the Data Gateway VM | Enforced | | 3 | No inbound firewall exceptions will be permitted for the Data Gateway VM | Enforced | | 4 | Conditional Access policies will be implemented for all user access to Microsoft Fabric services | Enforced | ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric and On-Premises Data Gateway: Formal Definitions **Microsoft Fabric** is an end-to-end, unified analytics platform from Microsoft that brings together data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering. It provides a secure, scalable environment for ingesting, transforming, and analyzing data from various sources. **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)** is a Microsoft component that acts as a secure bridge, enabling cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI to access data stored on-premises without opening inbound ports. The gateway establishes outbound, encrypted connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints, ensuring data remains protected during transit. ### OPDG Architecture and Responsibilities Enfrasys Solutions shall design the Data Gateway architecture and provide the configuration blueprint. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall provision the required on-premises Virtual Machine according to the specifications below and facilitate network access as per the security rules. #### Data Gateway VM Specifications | Component | Specification | |-------------------|---------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | #### Outbound Connectivity Requirements The On-Premises Data Gateway VM must be permitted to initiate outbound connections to specific Microsoft services over HTTPS and HTTP, as detailed in the NSG rules below. ### Outbound NSG Rules | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | Installation | Install and register On-Premises Data Gateway agent on provisioned VM | UMS | Pending | | Connectivity Validation | Test outbound connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and authentication endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | ## 4.3 Conditional Access for Microsoft Fabric ### Microsoft Entra Conditional Access: Formal Definition **Microsoft Entra Conditional Access** is a policy-based security feature that evaluates signals such as user identity, device health, location, and risk before granting or denying access to Microsoft cloud resources. It enforces adaptive controls to protect sensitive data and ensure compliance. All access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI services by UMS users and external collaborators shall be governed by Conditional Access policies, ensuring only authorized and compliant connections are permitted. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Conditional Access enforcement for Microsoft Fabric user authentication. --- ## 4.4 Summary of Network Responsibilities - **Enfrasys Solutions**: Responsible for overall network architecture design, Data Gateway configuration blueprint, and connectivity validation. - **Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS)**: Responsible for provisioning and configuring the Data Gateway VM, applying outbound NSG rules, and installing the On-Premises Data Gateway agent. --- This network design ensures secure, reliable, and policy-driven connectivity between UMS’s on-premises data assets and Microsoft Fabric/Power BI cloud services. # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access mappings for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environment. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS offering. Fabric is tightly integrated with Power BI, enabling organizations to manage and analyze data at scale with robust security and governance. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged position responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and security of the Microsoft Fabric environment. The Fabric Admin has the following responsibilities: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Environment Configuration | Manage global Fabric settings, enable/disable features, and configure tenant-wide options. | | User and Workspace Management | Assign roles, manage workspace creation, and oversee user access across the tenant. | | Security and Compliance | Enforce data governance, compliance policies, and monitor audit logs. | | Resource Monitoring | Track usage, performance, and optimize resource allocation. | | Integration Oversight | Approve and manage connections to external data sources and integrations. | Enfrasys Solutions shall define the Fabric Administrator role and its permissions. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign qualified personnel to this role. ## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric provides several native roles to support diverse analytics and data management workloads. The following roles are recommended for UMS: | Workspace Role | Description | |---------------|-------------| | Data Engineer | Responsible for designing, building, and maintaining data pipelines, datasets, and integrations within Fabric workspaces. | | Business User Admin | Manages business user access, workspace membership, and ensures business requirements are translated into data solutions. | | Data Analyst | Develops reports, dashboards, and analytical models using Power BI and Fabric datasets. | | Stakeholder | Consumes reports and dashboards, provides feedback, and participates in decision-making based on data insights. | | Project Director | Oversees project delivery, ensures alignment with strategic objectives, and monitors progress across analytics initiatives. | Enfrasys Solutions shall define and document the responsibilities and capabilities for each native role. UMS shall assign personnel to these roles according to project and operational needs. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access The following table maps workspace roles to suggested responsibilities and the designated UMS Person In Charge (PIC).
