It includes components such as Data Factory (for data integration and orchestration), Synapse (for advanced analytics and big data processing), and OneLake (a unified data lake). Fabric enables organizations to build end-to-end analytics solutions with seamless data movement, governance, and security. ### Power BI Overview Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise business intelligence platform that enables users to visualize data, share insights, and make informed decisions. Power BI integrates with Microsoft Fabric, allowing direct connectivity to data stored in OneLake and supporting advanced semantic modeling and visualization. ### Data Workflow Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |-------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the Bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the Silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python). | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data; results stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented. | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data ingestion layer | Use Fabric Data Factory pipelines to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server to Bronze Lakehouse. | | 2 | Layered Lakehouse architecture | Implement Bronze (raw), Silver (cleansed), and Gold (curated) Lakehouse layers for data modeling. | | 3 | Data transformation and cleansing | Utilize Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks (PySpark/Python), and Visual Query for transformation tasks. | | 4 | Data movement between Lakehouses | Use Dataflow Gen2 for movement between Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers. | | 5 | Machine Learning approach | Adopt primarily Manual ML using Fabric Notebooks; AutoML for demonstration purposes. | | 6 | Model management | Register trained models in Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and reuse. | | 7 | Scoring and output | Store scored outputs and predictions in the Gold Lakehouse. | | 8 | Data modeling and visualization | Build Semantic Models on Gold Lakehouse; connect Power BI via OneLake Direct Lake. | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration with Fabric and Power BI. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Conditional Access Conditional Access is a policy-based security feature in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) that controls access to applications and resources based on user signals, device state, location, and risk. It enforces requirements such as MFA, device compliance, or network restrictions before granting access. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access policy flow for Microsoft Fabric. #### Conditional Access Policy Decisions | No. | Conditional Access | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | #### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Since Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s tenant is not eligible for Conditional Access (due to licensing constraints), Enfrasys Solutions recommends enforcing Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the tenant level to secure access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources. ### Access Architecture ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 4: Secure access to UMS Fabric using Conditional Access and MFA. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size for Data Gateway The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric cloud services. Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the Data Gateway agent on a dedicated VM with the following specifications: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 5: Data Gateway architecture bridging on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends structuring Azure resources using Management Groups to align with Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s organizational hierarchy and governance requirements. Management Groups provide a logical container for managing access, policies, and compliance across multiple subscriptions and resource groups, ensuring consistent governance and simplified administration. ### Naming and Tagging Standards #### Azure Resource Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|-----------------| | Resource Group | rg | ums-fabric-rg | | Virtual Machine | vm | ums-opdg-vm01 | | Storage Account | st | umsdata01st | | Network Security Group | nsg | ums-fabric-nsg | #### Microsoft Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming -- | |-----|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK
Etc… | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF
Etc.. | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL
Etc.. | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Definition | |--------------|---------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Considerations ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions will design the governance framework; Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall operationalize and review governance disciplines as follows. #### Cost Management Tools | Azure Tool | Description | Cost Management Discipline | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------| | Azure Cost Management| Native Azure tool for monitoring and analyzing cloud spend | Cost Control, Optimization| | Azure Advisor | Provides cost optimization recommendations | Cost Optimization | | Azure Policy | Enforces tagging and resource configuration compliance | Cost Allocation, Compliance| #### Cost Management Review | Cost Item | Owner | Review Frequency | |---------------------|----------------------|---------------------| | Azure Subscription | UMS | Monthly | | Fabric Capacity | UMS | Quarterly | | Data Gateway VM | UMS | Monthly | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |----------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|-----------| | Resource without required tags | Notify resource owner; enforce tagging policy | UMS | | Cost threshold exceeded for subscription/capacity | Alert finance/admin team; review and optimize usage | UMS | | Unauthorized access attempt detected | Investigate and report; enforce MFA | UMS | --- Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design, initial configuration, and knowledge transfer; Universiti Malaysia Sabah is responsible for operationalization, monitoring, and ongoing governance. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section outlines the security architecture, network security group (NSG) rules, and encryption standards for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Data Gateway environment. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are essential security constructs that allow or deny network traffic to Azure resources in a virtual network. NSGs contain security rules that filter inbound and outbound traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions has designed the following NSG rules for the Data Gateway Virtual Machine (VM) to ensure secure connectivity and compliance with Microsoft best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement these rules as part of the deployment. ### Inbound Rules for Data Gateway VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |---------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_RDP_Admin | 100 | UMS Admin Subnet | * | VM Private IP | 3389 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_Health_Probe | 110 | Azure Load Balancer | * | VM Private IP | 65500-65534 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All_Inbound | 4096 | Any | * | Any | * | Any | Deny | ### Outbound Rules for Data Gateway VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | | Deny_All_Outbound | 4096 | Any | * | Any | * | Any | Deny | ## 7.2 Encryption Design ### Microsoft Fabric Encryption Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that provides data integration, engineering, warehousing, and business intelligence capabilities. All data stored in Microsoft Fabric is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default, and customers may optionally use customer-managed keys for enhanced control. Data in transit between Fabric services and client endpoints is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. ### Power BI Encryption Power BI is an interactive data visualization and business intelligence platform. Power BI encrypts data at rest using Microsoft-managed keys and supports customer-managed keys for Premium capacities. Data in transit is secured using TLS encryption. ### On-Premises Data Gateway Encryption The On-Premises Data Gateway facilitates secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services. The gateway encrypts all data in transit using HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) and does not store data at rest on the gateway VM. ### Security Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------| | Security Architecture Design | Define NSG rules, encryption standards, and access policies | Enfrasys Solutions | Ongoing| | Implementation of NSG Rules | Apply inbound and outbound NSG rules to Data Gateway VM | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending| | Encryption Configuration | Ensure all services (Fabric, Power BI, Data Gateway) use Microsoft-managed or customer-managed encryption | Universiti Malaysia Sabah | Pending| | Security Review and Validation | Review and validate security configurations against design and compliance requirements | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned| ## 7.3 Conditional Access and MFA Conditional Access is a policy-based approach in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) to enforce access controls based on user identity, location, device compliance, and risk signals. Due to licensing constraints, Universiti Malaysia Sabah is not eligible for Conditional Access at this stage. Therefore, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level to enhance account security. | No. | Conditional Access Policy Decision | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01| Configure policy in Conditional Access for Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Storage | | DD02| Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | | DD03| UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | | DD04| Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\dbf59fbb-e022-4e52-bced-891a290e7f85\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access policy enforcement for Microsoft Fabric authentication. ## 7.4 Security Implementation Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|----------| | Pre-Implementation (D-7) | Finalize security design and validate NSG and encryption requirements | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Implementation (D0) | Apply NSG rules, configure MFA, and validate encryption settings | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | | Post-Implementation (D+1) | Security review, monitoring, and remediation of any identified issues | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured deployment and migration methodology for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory actions, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The following table details all preparatory activities, responsible parties, and status prior to migration commencement. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment phase to validate the migration approach and minimize risk. The following steps shall be executed: 1. **Pilot User/Department Selection:** UMS shall nominate a representative user group or department for the pilot. 2. **Pilot Data Migration:** Enfrasys Solutions will coordinate the migration of a subset of data from the staging area database to the Microsoft Fabric environment. 3. **Connectivity Validation:** Enfrasys Solutions will verify the On-Premises Data Gateway connectivity and data pipeline integrity. 4. **User Acceptance Testing (UAT):** UMS will conduct UAT on migrated workloads and provide sign-off. 5. **Issue Resolution:** Enfrasys Solutions will address any issues identified during the pilot. ## 8.3 Production The production rollout will follow a phased timeline approach to ensure business continuity and minimize disruption. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|-------------| | D-7 | Finalize migration schedule and communication plan with all stakeholders | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D-7 | Complete backup of source systems | UMS | Pending | | D-3 | Freeze changes to source data warehouse (as agreed) | UMS | Pending | | D0 | Execute production data migration from staging area to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys | Pending | | D0 | Validate data integrity and system connectivity | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+1 | Enable user access to production environment | UMS | Pending | | D+1 | Monitor system performance and user feedback | Enfrasys | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support The following support and handover activities will be conducted to ensure a successful transition and operational stability. | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------------|-------------| | 1 | Post-migration monitoring and issue remediation | Enfrasys | Pending | | 2 | Knowledge transfer and documentation handover | Enfrasys | Pending | | 3 | Final acceptance and project closure | UMS | Pending | | 4 | Ongoing operational support (as per agreement) | Enfrasys | Pending | # 9.0 Appendix ## 9.1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: Microsoft Learn – Virtual Machines Overview [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Title: Microsoft Learn – Azure Compute Documentation [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/compute/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/compute/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: Azure Subscription and Service Limits, Quotas, and Constraints [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Title: Azure VM Sizes and Families [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) ### Others Title: Azure VM Availability and SLA [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability) Title: Azure Compute Reserved Instances [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reservations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reservations) ## This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric service, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents an Azure Resource Group that contains and organizes resources related to the UMS Fabric service.
   - The Resource Group acts as a logical container for managing and deploying resources in Azure.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely provides specific functionality or services that external users need to access.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric service.
   - Ensures that only authorized external users can access the service based on predefined policies (e.g., user identity, device compliance, location, etc.).

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an individual or entity outside the organization attempting to access the UMS Fabric service.
   - Access is mediated through Conditional Access policies to ensure security and compliance.

5. **Data Flow:**
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the UMS Fabric service to the external user.
   - Conditional Access acts as a gatekeeper, enforcing security policies before granting access to the external user.

This architecture emphasizes secure access to cloud-hosted services using Azure's Conditional Access capabilities.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

1. **User Authentication**:
   - The process begins with a **user** attempting to authenticate to **Microsoft Fabric**.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:
   - The authentication request is evaluated against **Entra conditional access policies**. This step involves:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are analyzed. Examples include:
       - **Users and groups**: Identifying the user or group attempting access.
       - **Network location**: Determining the geographical or network context of the request.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, allowing or denying access based on the policy.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:
   - If access is granted, the user is directed to **Microsoft Fabric**, which includes various components:
     - **Microsoft Fabric Services**: Represented by icons for services such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence.
     - **OneLake**: A unified data lake for storing and managing data within Microsoft Fabric.

This diagram illustrates the flow of user authentication and conditional access enforcement before accessing Microsoft Fabric services and resources.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics workflow within the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, leveraging Azure Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI. Below is a detailed description of the components and their interactions:

1. **UMS Staging Area (Microsoft SQL Server 2017 – Data Warehouse)**:
   - Acts as the source system where raw data is stored.
   - Data is ingested from this staging area into the pipeline.

2. **Data Factory**:
   - Facilitates the ingestion of data from the UMS Staging Area into the processing pipeline.
   - Orchestrates the movement and transformation of data through various stages.

3. **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the first layer of data processing.
   - Stores raw ingested data in a structured format for further processing.

4. **XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the second layer of data processing.
   - Contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for advanced analytics or further transformations.

5. **Data Science**:
   - A component where advanced analytics, machine learning, or data modeling is performed.
   - Works on the processed data from the Silver layer to derive insights or predictions.

6. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the final layer of data processing.
   - Stores highly curated, business-ready data for reporting and visualization.

7. **Identity_SM**:
   - Likely a service or module for identity management or security, ensuring secure access to the Gold layer data.

8. **OneLake**:
   - A unified data lake storage solution that integrates with the pipeline.
   - Provides seamless data access and storage for downstream systems.

9. **Power BI**:
   - Connects to the Gold layer via OneLake.
   - Used for data visualization and reporting, enabling business users to derive insights from the processed data.

### Data Flow:
- Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area into the pipeline using Azure Data Factory.
- The data flows sequentially through the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers, undergoing transformations and enrichment at each stage.
- Advanced analytics and machine learning are applied in the Data Science component.
- The final curated data in the Gold layer is securely accessed via Identity_SM and stored in OneLake.
- Power BI connects to OneLake to visualize and report on the processed data.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data processing pipeline leveraging Azure services for end-to-end data management and analytics.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - Acts as the source or destination for data being processed.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Serves as a secure bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Ensures secure, encrypted communication for data flow.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data.
   - Likely used for advanced analytics, data processing, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows bidirectionally between the SQL Server database and the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway establishes an outbound secure connection to the UMS Fabric, ensuring no inbound ports are required for security purposes.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations, analytics, or storage in the cloud.

This image represents a **technical architecture diagram** illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encapsulating the data and machine learning processes.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - This data serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - The data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an **AutoML Experiment**.
     - The experiment automates the machine learning pipeline, including feature engineering, model selection, and training.
     - The results are scored using a **Notebook**, which likely involves custom evaluation or post-processing.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - Alternatively, the data is processed through a **Manual Machine Learning** pipeline.
     - This involves custom modeling and experimentation using a **Notebook** for greater control over the process.

4. **Output: XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - The results from either AutoML or Manual Machine Learning are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, representing the refined and processed data layer (Gold tables).

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A component that likely handles identity mapping, security, or metadata enrichment for the processed data.

6. **Integration with Power BI**:
   - The XX_Gold_LK Tables are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake** for visualization and reporting.
   - This enables business users to derive insights from the processed data.

### Summary:
The diagram outlines a flexible machine learning workflow where data from OneLake (Silver tables) is processed through either automated or manual machine learning pipelines. The refined data (Gold tables) is then enriched and visualized in Power BI, enabling end-to-end data processing and analytics.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution design, outlining the project’s context, objectives, and the purpose and audience of this document. ## 2.1 Project Overview UMS has initiated a strategic project to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric. The project scope encompasses the end-to-end lifecycle of data: from ingestion, transformation, and advanced analytics to business visualization. Key objectives include: - Establishing a Microsoft Fabric workspace tailored to UMS’s business requirements. - Developing robust ETL pipelines using Fabric Data Factory for seamless data integration and orchestration. - Utilizing Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing. - Enabling machine learning applications via the Data Science capabilities within Microsoft Fabric. - Integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics and actionable insights. This initiative is designed to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with the latest Microsoft Fabric services, ensuring scalability, agility, and data-driven decision-making. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The intent of this Solution Design Document is to formally define the architecture, technology components, design decisions, and implementation approach for the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric project. This document serves as the authoritative reference throughout the project lifecycle, guiding solution implementation, governance, and future enhancements. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended audience for this document includes: - UMS IT Department - UMS Business Stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team Responsibility for document review and sign-off is jointly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. Both parties shall ensure the accuracy, completeness, and alignment of the solution design with UMS’s business and technical requirements. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, establishing the foundational technologies for UMS’s modern data platform. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is a unified, end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) solution. Microsoft Fabric is designed to simplify and accelerate the process of transforming raw data into actionable insights by integrating core services such as Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Science. - **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory provides robust data integration and orchestration capabilities, enabling organizations to ingest, transform, and move data across a variety of sources and destinations with ease and reliability. - **Synapse**: Microsoft Fabric Synapse delivers advanced data analytics and big data processing, supporting large-scale data warehousing, real-time analytics, and complex data transformations. - **Data Science**: The Data Science service within Microsoft Fabric empowers organizations to build, train, and deploy machine learning models directly within the platform, supporting modern AI-driven analytics. Microsoft Fabric’s unified approach enables organizations like UMS to streamline data workflows, improve collaboration, and accelerate time-to-insight across all stages of the data lifecycle. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s industry-leading business intelligence (BI) platform, designed to enable organizations to visualize data, share insights, and make informed business decisions. Power BI seamlessly integrates with Microsoft Fabric, allowing users to connect to curated data sets, build interactive reports and dashboards, and distribute analytics across the organization. Power BI’s capabilities include: - Data visualization through interactive dashboards and reports. - Integration with Microsoft Fabric workspaces for secure, governed access to enterprise data. - Self-service analytics, empowering business users to explore data and generate insights without IT dependency. Through its integration with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI ensures that UMS can leverage a single, secure platform for end-to-end data analytics, from ingestion and transformation to visualization and business decision-making. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s comprehensive cloud-based identity and access management solution. Entra ID provides secure authentication, single sign-on, and role-based access control for users and applications across Microsoft 365, Azure, and Microsoft Fabric environments. Entra ID’s role in the UMS data platform includes: - Centralized identity management for all users and administrators. - Secure access to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and integrated services. - Enforcement of organizational policies, conditional access, and compliance requirements. By leveraging Microsoft Entra ID, UMS ensures robust security, streamlined user access, and consistent governance across all components of the data analytics platform. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture for UMS, including connectivity between on-premises and Microsoft Fabric, and the deployment of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a unified SaaS experience. It is designed to connect seamlessly with both cloud and on-premises data sources. UMS’s network topology is designed to securely connect on-premises resources to Microsoft Fabric in Azure. The architecture leverages outbound-only connectivity from the on-premises environment to the cloud, ensuring no inbound ports are exposed. At this stage, Enfrasys recommends not implementing Azure Private Link due to the following rationale: - The current data flow requirements are met by secure outbound connections via the On-Premises Data Gateway. - Private Link introduces additional complexity and operational overhead, which is not justified for the initial deployment phase. - UMS can revisit Private Link adoption in future phases as data sensitivity or compliance requirements evolve. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\bed1e709-49bf-4a98-b8bc-e44b761f1c04\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Secure data flow between on-premises SQL Server, OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure, lightweight bridge that enables secure data transfer between on-premises data sources and Microsoft cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Enfrasys Solutions shall deploy the OPDG agent within a dedicated virtual machine (VM) in the UMS on-premises environment. The OPDG facilitates secure, encrypted outbound connectivity, ensuring that no inbound firewall rules are required. UMS shall be responsible for preparing the on-premises network, including ensuring outbound internet access to the required Microsoft endpoints. ### 4.2.1 OPDG VM Specification | Component | Specification | |-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### 4.2.2 Outbound Security Rules (NSG) The following Azure Network Security Group (NSG) outbound rules are required for the OPDG VM to operate securely and reliably: | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 4.3 Task and Rollout Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|--------| | OPDG VM Deployment | Provision VM, install Windows OS, configure network, join domain if required | UMS | Pending| | .NET Framework Installation | Install .NET Framework 4.8 on OPDG VM | UMS | Pending| | OPDG Agent Installation | Download and install On-Premises Data Gateway agent on the provisioned VM | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending| | Outbound NSG Configuration | Configure outbound security rules as per specifications | UMS | Pending| | Connectivity Validation | Test and validate secure connectivity from OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric endpoints | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending| ## 4.4 Summary of Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------| | 1 | Use outbound-only connectivity via OPDG for on-premises to cloud integration | Approved | | 2 | Defer implementation of Azure Private Link for initial deployment phase | Approved | | 3 | Deploy OPDG agent on dedicated Windows Server VM with specified resources | Approved | | 4 | Enforce required outbound NSG rules for secure and reliable operation | Approved | | 5 | Assign deployment and configuration responsibilities to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS as specified | Approved | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric & Power BI This section defines the role structure, permissions, and assignment responsibilities for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within the UMS environment. ## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence (BI) under a unified SaaS experience. It enables organizations to manage and analyze data across multiple sources using a single, secure platform. The Fabric Administrator is a privileged role responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and management of Microsoft Fabric. This role typically includes the following responsibilities and permissions: | Capability | Description | |------------|-------------| | Tenant Settings Management | Configure and enforce tenant-wide settings for security, data access, and compliance. | | Workspace Management | Create, update, and delete workspaces; assign workspace roles. | | Data Pipeline Oversight | Monitor, schedule, and troubleshoot data pipelines and integration flows. | | Resource Provisioning | Allocate and manage Fabric capacities and resources. | | Security and Compliance | Implement data protection policies, manage access controls, and audit activities. | | User and Role Administration | Assign roles, manage user access, and oversee workspace permissions. | Enfrasys Solutions shall be responsible for mapping the Fabric Administrator role to appropriate UMS personnel. UMS shall assign users to the mapped roles as per the approved access matrix. ## 5.2 Microsoft-native Roles Power BI and Microsoft Fabric provide several native workspace-level roles, each with specific permissions and access scopes. These roles ensure proper segregation of duties and least-privilege access. | Permission | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer | |------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------| | Add/Remove Users | Yes | No | No | No | | Publish Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Edit Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Delete Content | Yes | Yes | No | No | | View Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Schedule Refresh | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Share Content | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | Manage Workspace Settings | Yes | No | No | No | **Role Descriptions:** - **Admin:** Full control over workspace, including user management and settings. - **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and delete content but cannot manage users or workspace settings. - **Member:** Can view and interact with content, and contribute to reports and datasets. - **Viewer:** Read-only access to content within the workspace. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS assign roles based on the principle of least privilege, ensuring users have only the permissions necessary for their responsibilities. ## 5.3 UMS Workspace Role and Access Enfrasys Solutions has mapped the following workspace roles to suggested UMS personnel. UMS shall ensure timely assignment of users to the designated roles to maintain operational integrity and compliance. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Design and Decision This section details the design, considerations, and decisions for the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI implementation for UMS, covering data workflows, access, platform requirements, resource organization, and governance. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. It leverages OneLake as a unified data lake and provides seamless integration with Power BI for advanced analytics and visualization. ### Power BI Definition Power BI is a suite of business analytics tools by Microsoft for analyzing data and sharing insights. It enables the creation of interactive reports and dashboards, connecting to a wide range of data sources, and integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric for visualization and semantic modeling. ### Data Workflow Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |-------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\bed1e709-49bf-4a98-b8bc-e44b761f1c04\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine Learning workflow in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Microsoft Entra ID Definition Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing secure authentication, single sign-on, and advanced access policies such as Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access #### Considerations - Azure Cost Management ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/ ### Limits and Boundaries Title: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/costs/understand-cost-mgt-data ### Others Title: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-cost-management-and/ct-p/AzureCostManagement # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security controls and encryption mechanisms for the UMS Data Gateway VM and all data flows involving Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a core Azure security feature that enables filtering of network traffic to and from Azure resources in an Azure Virtual Network. NSGs contain security rules that allow or deny inbound and outbound traffic based on source or destination IP address, port, and protocol. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following NSG rules for the Data Gateway VM to ensure secure and compliant connectivity with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. ### Outbound NSG Rules for Data Gateway VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |------------------------------|----------|-------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160 | VM Private IP Addresses | * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the above NSG rules on the Data Gateway VM. UMS shall monitor compliance and review NSG rule effectiveness as part of ongoing security governance. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a fundamental requirement for protecting data in transit and at rest within the Microsoft Fabric and Power BI ecosystem. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following encryption mechanisms for all relevant data flows. ### 7.2.1 Encryption in Transit - **On-Premises to Microsoft Fabric/Power BI:** All data transmitted from the on-premises Data Gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints is protected using HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher). This ensures confidentiality and integrity of data during transit over public and private networks. - **Within OneLake and Microsoft Fabric:** Internal data movement within Microsoft Fabric and OneLake utilizes Microsoft-managed encryption for all service communications, leveraging TLS for all API and service-to-service traffic. - **Power BI Service:** Data sent to and from the Power BI service is encrypted in transit using HTTPS, ensuring secure access for users and service integrations. ### 7.2.2 Encryption at Rest - **OneLake Storage:** All data stored in OneLake is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys (AES 256-bit encryption), compliant with Azure Storage encryption standards. - **Microsoft Fabric and Power BI:** Data assets stored within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are encrypted at rest using Microsoft’s default encryption mechanisms, with options for customer-managed keys if required for compliance. Enfrasys Solutions shall design and implement the encryption configurations for all data flows as described above. UMS shall be responsible for monitoring encryption compliance and maintaining records for audit and regulatory requirements. # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach for deployment and migration activities for UMS, including pre-migration preparation, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The following table details all preparatory tasks required before migration activities commence. Ownership and status are clearly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|---------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment to validate the migration strategy and ensure minimal disruption to UMS operations. The pilot will cover a representative subset of workloads and users. **Pilot Deployment Steps:** 1. Provision the Microsoft Fabric environment for pilot users. 2. Configure and test the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) connectivity. 3. Migrate a sample dataset from the staging area database. 4. Validate data integrity and report functionality with UMS pilot users. 5. Collect feedback and resolve any identified issues. **Pilot Acceptance Criteria:** - Successful connectivity between on-premises data sources and Microsoft Fabric. - Accurate migration of sample datasets with no data loss. - Functionality of reports and dashboards verified by UMS stakeholders. - UMS sign-off on pilot results. ## 8.3 Production The production rollout will follow a structured timeline to minimize risk and ensure clear accountability. Enfrasys Solutions will execute all migration and deployment tasks, while UMS will validate the environment and provide final acceptance. ### Production Rollout Timeline | Task | Owner | Date | |------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------| | Pre-Migration Verification (D-7) | Enfrasys | D-7 | | User Communication & Change Management (D-5) | UMS | D-5 | | Final Data Backup (D-1) | UMS | D-1 | | Production Cutover (D0) | Enfrasys | D0 | | Post-Cutover Validation (D+1) | UMS | D+1 | | Issue Remediation & Support (D+1 to D+7) | Enfrasys | D+1 to D+7 | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Upon completion of migration activities, Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-migration support, including monitoring and escalation procedures. - **Support Handover:** Enfrasys Solutions will formally transition operational responsibility to UMS after successful post-migration validation. - **Monitoring:** Enfrasys Solutions will monitor the Microsoft Fabric environment for performance, data integrity, and connectivity issues during the hypercare period (D+1 to D+7). - **Escalation Process:** Any critical issues identified by UMS shall be escalated to Enfrasys Solutions for immediate resolution. UMS will be provided with escalation contacts and response timelines. - **Knowledge Transfer:** Enfrasys Solutions will conduct a knowledge transfer session for UMS administrators, covering environment management, monitoring, and troubleshooting procedures. UMS shall be responsible for validating the environment and confirming operational readiness post-migration. # 9.0 Appendix This section provides reference material, technical constraints, and links to official Microsoft documentation for each major solution domain. Add relevant technical details, constraints, and references as needed. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Compute Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Azure VM Sizes: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) Azure Shared Responsibility Model: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/shared-responsibility](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/shared-responsibility) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Subscription and Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Azure Compute Quotas: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/quotas](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/quotas) ### Others Azure Availability Sets and Zones: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability-set-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability-set-overview) Azure Disk Types: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Virtual Network Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) Azure Network Security Groups: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Networking Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-faq](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-faq) Azure ExpressRoute Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-limits) ### Others Azure Firewall Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/firewall/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/firewall/overview) Azure Private Link: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-link-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/private-link-overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Microsoft Entra ID Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/) Identity Security Best Practices: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/identity-management-best-practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/identity-management-best-practices) ### Limits and Boundaries Entra ID Service Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/groups-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/groups-limits) Conditional Access Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/technical-reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/technical-reference) ### Others Azure Key Vault Documentation: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview) Privileged Identity Management: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Azure Monitor Overview: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Log Analytics Workspace: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview) ### Limits and Boundaries Azure Monitor Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-limits) Diagnostic Settings Limits: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ### Others Azure Activity Log: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/activity-log) Azure Alerts: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview) ## This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

The diagram illustrates a system architecture involving Azure Resource Groups, a UMS Fabric service, and Conditional Access for external users. The components and their interactions are as follows:

1. **Fabric-RG (Azure Resource Group):**
   - Represents an Azure Resource Group that contains and organizes resources related to the UMS Fabric service.
   - The Resource Group acts as a logical container for managing and deploying resources in Azure.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - A service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Likely provides specific functionality or services that external users need to access.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric service.
   - Ensures that only authorized users meeting specific conditions (e.g., location, device compliance, MFA) can access the service.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an individual or entity outside the organization attempting to access the UMS Fabric service.
   - Access is mediated through Conditional Access policies.

**Data Flow:**
- The external user initiates a request to access the UMS Fabric service.
- The request is evaluated against Conditional Access policies.
- If the conditions are met, access is granted, and the user can interact with the UMS Fabric service hosted in the Fabric-RG.

This architecture emphasizes secure access control for external users while leveraging Azure's resource management and security capabilities.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, either granting or denying access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric and OneLake**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric provides a suite of tools and services, represented by icons for various components such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence.  
   - OneLake is highlighted as a central data lake within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and analytics workflow within the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, leveraging Azure Data Factory, OneLake, and Power BI. Below is a detailed breakdown of the components and their interactions:

1. **Data Source (UMS Staging Area)**:
   - The process begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017-based data warehouse, referred to as the "UMS Staging Area."

2. **Data Factory (Ingestion)**:
   - Azure Data Factory is used to ingest data from the staging area into the processing pipeline.

3. **Bronze Layer (XX_Bronze_LK Tables)**:
   - The ingested raw data is stored in the Bronze layer, represented as "XX_Bronze_LK Tables." This layer serves as the initial landing zone for raw, unprocessed data.

4. **Silver Layer (XX_Silver_LK Tables)**:
   - Data from the Bronze layer is transformed and refined into the Silver layer, represented as "XX_Silver_LK Tables." This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further processing.

5. **Data Science and Transformation**:
   - Azure Data Factory and Data Science tools are used to perform advanced transformations, analytics, and machine learning on the Silver layer data.

6. **Gold Layer (XX_Gold_LK Tables)**:
   - The processed and curated data is stored in the Gold layer, represented as "XX_Gold_LK Tables." This layer contains high-quality, business-ready data.

7. **Identity_SM**:
   - A component labeled "Identity_SM" is integrated into the Gold layer, likely for identity management, security, or metadata tagging.

8. **OneLake Integration**:
   - The data pipeline is integrated with OneLake, a unified data lake solution, enabling seamless data storage and access.

9. **Power BI (Visualization)**:
   - The curated data in the Gold layer is connected to Power BI via OneLake for visualization and reporting, enabling business intelligence and decision-making.

10. **UMS Fabric**:
    - The entire workflow is encapsulated within the "UMS Fabric," indicating a unified data platform or framework for managing the data lifecycle.

This architecture demonstrates a modern data pipeline leveraging Azure services for data ingestion, transformation, storage, and visualization.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - Acts as the source or destination for data processing.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Serves as a secure bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and cloud-based services.
   - Facilitates data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Ensures secure, encrypted communication for data flow.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that interacts with the on-premises data.
   - Likely used for advanced data processing, analytics, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows bidirectionally between the SQL Server database and the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway establishes an outbound secure connection to the UMS Fabric, ensuring no inbound ports are required.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations, analytics, or storage in the cloud.

This architecture ensures secure, seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem. Below is a detailed description of the components and their interactions:

1. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - Acts as the overarching framework or environment for the workflow. It encapsulates the data flow and machine learning processes.

2. **Data Source - XX_Silver_LK Tables**:  
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**, a unified data storage layer.  
   - This data serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:  
   - **AutoML**:  
     - Automates the machine learning process.  
     - The **AutoML Experiment** component processes the input data from the Silver tables.  
     - The results are scored using a notebook, and the output is passed to the next stage.  
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:  
     - Allows for custom modeling using notebooks.  
     - The input data from the Silver tables is processed manually, and the resulting model is passed to the next stage.

4. **XX_Gold_LK Tables**:  
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where the processed and scored results are stored.  
   - This layer is used for further analysis and reporting.

5. **Identity_SM**:  
   - A component that likely handles identity management, security, or metadata tagging for the Gold tables.

6. **Power BI Integration**:  
   - The Gold tables are connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake** for visualization and reporting.  
   - This enables business users to derive insights from the processed data.

7. **OneLake**:  
   - Serves as the central data repository, facilitating seamless data storage and access across the workflow.

### Data Flow:
- Data originates from the **XX_Silver_LK Tables** in OneLake.  
- It is processed either through **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning**.  
- The processed data is stored in the **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.  
- The Gold tables are enriched with identity or metadata via **Identity_SM**.  
- Finally, the data is visualized in **Power BI**, connected through OneLake.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
