# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the Power BI and Microsoft Fabric solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). ## 2.1 Project Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is embarking on a strategic initiative to modernize its data analytics capabilities through the implementation of Microsoft Power BI and Microsoft Fabric. The scope of this project encompasses the design and deployment of a comprehensive data workflow, covering data ingestion, transformation, and visualization tailored to UMS’s business requirements. Key objectives include the establishment of a dedicated Fabric workspace, development of an end-to-end ETL pipeline, enablement of machine learning processes, and seamless integration with Power BI for actionable analytics. The solution leverages core Microsoft Fabric services, including Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science for machine learning applications, ensuring alignment with UMS’s cloud adoption strategy. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The purpose of this Solution Design Document is to formally capture the architectural decisions, technical specifications, and implementation approach for the Power BI and Microsoft Fabric deployment at UMS. This document serves as the authoritative reference for design review, stakeholder sign-off, and as a comprehensive guide for implementation and operationalization of the solution. ## 2.3 Document Audience This document is intended for the following audiences: - UMS IT Department - UMS Data Analytics Team - UMS Business Stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions Project Team Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting this document. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for reviewing and providing formal sign-off prior to project execution. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview This section provides formal definitions and context for Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID as foundational components of the Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) modern data platform. Content is prepared by Enfrasys Solutions for UMS validation. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform designed to unify data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. Microsoft Fabric consolidates core services, including Data Factory, Synapse, Data Science, and OneLake, to deliver a seamless, scalable, and secure data ecosystem. - **Data Factory**: A cloud-based data integration service that enables the creation, scheduling, and orchestration of data pipelines to ingest, prepare, and transform data from diverse sources. - **Synapse**: An advanced analytics service that combines big data and data warehousing, supporting SQL analytics, Spark, and real-time analytics workloads. - **Data Science**: Provides integrated machine learning and AI capabilities, including AutoML and notebook-based experimentation, to operationalize predictive analytics. - **OneLake**: A unified, enterprise-grade data lake storage solution that centralizes all organizational data, supporting lakehouse and warehouse paradigms. Microsoft Fabric’s role in the UMS data platform is to orchestrate the entire data lifecycle—from ingestion in the UMS Staging Area, through transformation and advanced analytics, to secure storage and business reporting. This enables Universiti Malaysia Sabah to standardize data workflows, ensure data quality, and accelerate time-to-insight. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture. ## 3.2 Power BI Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise business intelligence platform, delivering interactive data visualization, self-service analytics, and robust reporting capabilities. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, enabling seamless access to curated data in OneLake, semantic models, and advanced analytics outputs. Power BI’s integration with Microsoft Fabric allows UMS to: - Connect directly to gold-standard datasets and semantic models. - Build and share real-time dashboards and reports. - Empower users with governed, secure, and scalable analytics. Power BI supports both desktop and web-based authoring, and leverages the security and compliance features of the Microsoft ecosystem. For UMS, Power BI will serve as the primary interface for business users to consume insights derived from the data platform. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. Entra ID provides authentication, authorization, and conditional access for users, applications, and resources across Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and other cloud services. Entra ID enables Universiti Malaysia Sabah to: - Centrally manage user identities and credentials. - Enforce role-based access controls (RBAC) and security policies. - Integrate with on-premises directories and third-party identity providers. - Support single sign-on (SSO) and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for enhanced security. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for defining the integration and configuration of Entra ID within the UMS data platform. Universiti Malaysia Sabah shall validate and approve all identity and access management configurations prior to production rollout. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: Machine learning workflow and Power BI integration in Microsoft Fabric. # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network architecture and gateway design for integrating Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s (UMS) on-premises environment with Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview Universiti Malaysia Sabah’s network topology is designed to ensure secure, seamless connectivity between on-premises resources and Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The architecture leverages Azure Resource Groups, Conditional Access, and Entra ID for secure access management. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access and authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric. ### Network Topology Description - **On-Premises Environment:** UMS’s data warehouse operates on Microsoft SQL Server 2017, hosted within the university’s secure internal network. - **Cloud Environment:** Microsoft Fabric is provisioned within a dedicated Azure Resource Group (Fabric-RG). - **Connectivity:** The On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) establishes a secure outbound connection from UMS’s network to Microsoft Fabric, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules. - **Access Control:** All user and system access to Microsoft Fabric is governed by Entra Conditional Access policies, enforcing identity-based, location-aware, and device-compliant authentication. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 2: Data flow between UMS SQL Server, On-Premises Data Gateway, and UMS Fabric. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway ### Microsoft Fabric and On-Premises Data Gateway Definition **Microsoft Fabric** is Microsoft’s unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a single SaaS solution. It provides a secure, scalable environment for data storage, processing, and visualization. **On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)** is a Microsoft component that acts as a secure bridge, enabling cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric to access on-premises data sources without exposing internal networks to inbound connections. The OPDG agent runs as a Windows service and securely relays data between the on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric using outbound HTTPS connections. ### OPDG Design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah Enfrasys Solutions recommends the deployment of the On-Premises Data Gateway on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s data center. This gateway will facilitate secure, reliable connectivity from the on-premises SQL Server 2017 staging area to Microsoft Fabric. #### VM Specification Table | Component | Specification | |-------------------|---------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | #### Roles and Responsibilities | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------| | OPDG Architecture and Design | Solution design, sizing, and configuration standards | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | VM Provisioning and Infrastructure | Provision VM according to specifications, ensure network readiness | UMS | Planned | | OPDG Installation and Configuration | Deploy and configure OPDG agent, validate connectivity to SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | | Security and Firewall Configuration | Ensure outbound connectivity on required ports, no inbound rules needed | UMS | Planned | | End-to-End Connectivity Testing | Validate data flow from SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric via OPDG | Enfrasys Solutions | Planned | ### Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Data Gateway runs on a dedicated VM | Adopted | | 2 | Outbound-only connectivity from on-premises to Fabric | Adopted | | 3 | Minimum VM specification: 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, Windows 10/Server 2019, .NET 4.8 | Adopted | | 4 | UMS responsible for VM/infrastructure readiness; Enfrasys for gateway design | Adopted | ## 4.3 Design Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------| | 1 | Secure, outbound-only data transfer between on-premises and cloud | Data Integration | | 2 | High-availability and resilience for gateway VM | Data Gateway Operations | | 3 | Compliance with UMS’s internal security policies and Microsoft best practices| Security, Compliance | | 4 | Scalable VM resources to support anticipated data transfer volumes | Performance, Scalability | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged role responsible for managing and configuring the overall Fabric environment, including security, workspace provisioning, and compliance settings. **Responsibilities of the Fabric Administrator include:** - Managing Fabric capacities and workspaces. - Assigning and revoking workspace roles. - Overseeing compliance, security, and audit configurations. - Monitoring usage and performance. - Coordinating with Microsoft support for escalations. **Assignment:** Enfrasys Solutions shall define the Fabric Administrator role and its responsibilities. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign suitable personnel to the Fabric Administrator role and manage user lifecycle processes. ## 5.2 Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric and Power BI provide several native roles to support granular access control and workload separation. Each role is designed to align with specific responsibilities across data engineering, analytics, and administration. | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS PIC | |-----|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias

Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi | **Responsibility Assignment:** - Enfrasys Solutions shall define all workspace roles and provide guidance on best practices for role assignment. - Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall assign users to the designated roles and maintain user management, ensuring compliance with organizational policies and security standards. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Design and Decision This section documents the end-to-end solution design for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, from data ingestion to analytics and machine learning. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations ### Microsoft Fabric Overview Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end, unified analytics platform that brings together data integration, engineering, science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS offering. It provides a common data lake (OneLake), integrated pipelines, data engineering, and machine learning capabilities, enabling organizations to manage data workflows efficiently and securely. ### Power BI Overview Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that provides interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, enabling seamless data visualization and advanced analytics. ### Data Workflow Considerations | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Use Data Factory pipelines for orchestrating data ingestion from Microsoft SQL Server to OneLake Bronze Lakehouse. | Adopted | | DD02 | Implement multi-layered Lakehouse architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for data processing and curation. | Adopted | | DD03 | Use Dataflow Gen2, Notebooks, and Visual Query for data cleansing and transformation in the Silver layer. | Adopted | | DD04 | Utilize both AutoML and manual ML (Fabric Notebooks) for machine learning, with results stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Adopted | | DD05 | Register trained models in the Fabric Model Registry for lifecycle management and reuse. | Adopted | | DD06 | Power BI will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake Direct Lake for real-time data visualization. | Adopted | | DD07 | On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) will be deployed for secure data transfer from on-premises SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric. | Adopted | | DD08 | Conditional Access and MFA to be enforced for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. | Enforced (with MFA at tenant level due to licensing constraints) | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow in Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions ### Microsoft Entra ID Overview Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service, providing secure authentication, conditional access, and seamless integration with Microsoft 365 and Azure services. ### Conditional Access Conditional Access policies in Microsoft Entra ID allow organizations to enforce automated access controls based on user, location, device, and risk signals. #### Summary UMS currently does not possess the required Microsoft Entra ID P1/P2 licenses to enable Conditional Access. As a result, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is enforced at the tenant level for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Conditional Access policies are recommended for future implementation pending license acquisition. | No. | Conditional Access | |------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage. | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy. | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts. | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment. | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 3: Conditional Access and MFA enforcement flow for Microsoft Fabric. ### Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) #### Summary MFA is enforced at the tenant level for all UMS users to enhance security and mitigate unauthorized access risks. This is a mandatory control in the absence of Conditional Access licensing. ### Entra ID Integration #### Summary All user authentication and access to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI are managed via Microsoft Entra ID, ensuring centralized identity management, auditability, and future extensibility for advanced access policies. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size for Data Gateway | Component | Specification | |-------------------|----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 4: Data Gateway integration between on-premises SQL Server and Microsoft Fabric. ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### Management Groups Enfrasys Solutions recommends Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) adopt a logical Azure management group structure to organize and govern resources efficiently. The structure should be as follows: - Root Management Group: UMS-Tenant - Data & Analytics Management Group - Fabric-RG (Resource Group for Microsoft Fabric workloads) This structure enables centralized policy enforcement, cost tracking, and compliance management. ### Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |----------------|--------------|----------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Virtual Machine| VM | Gateway-VM | | Storage Account| ST | umsdatastg01 | #### Microsoft Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|------------------|------------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronym Table | Abbreviation | Description | |--------------|--------------------------| | RG | Resource Group | | VM | Virtual Machine | | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### Governance Disciplines Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following governance disciplines for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments: - Cost Management and Optimization - Resource Organization and Tagging - Security and Access Control - Compliance and Auditability - Data Lifecycle Management #### Cost Management Table | Cost Item | Owner | Review Frequency | |----------------------|----------------------|---------------------| | Fabric Capacity | UMS | Monthly | | VM Consumption | UMS | Monthly | | Power BI Licensing | UMS | Quarterly | | Data Storage (OneLake)| UMS | Monthly | ### Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |---------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|------------------| | Unauthorized access attempt | Review logs, enforce MFA, notify security | UMS | | Resource naming/tagging non-compliance | Remediate resource name/tag, notify owner | UMS | | Cost overrun on Fabric Capacity or VM | Alert stakeholders, review usage | UMS | | Data retention policy violation | Enforce retention, escalate to compliance | UMS | ## 6.7 Workspace Role Mapping | No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | UMS Personnel | |-----|---------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Workspace Admin | Fabric Admin / Data Engineer | Dr Suraya Binti Alias, Sektor Analitik Data DARTIC, PPDM Official, Mohd. Zamrie Bin Musa, Noor Afiza Binti Momen, Ts AG Azihar @ AG KU Azihar Bin AG Tangah | | 2 | Workspace Contributor | Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Mohd Khairul Nizam Bin Senu (DB), Assie Zaee Petrus (DB), Muhaimin Bin Mad Noor (Infra), Nuraliah Binti Alias (Infra), Chong Tze Chien (Server Security), Abdul Latif Matali (Server Security) | | 3 | Workspace Viewer | Data Analyst / Stakeholder / Project Director | Director Dr Farashazillah Binti Yahya, Stakeholders: Roslee Bin Talip, Dayangku Rozlina BTE PG. Aliuddin, Norhanieyati Binti Norhaizat, Zaidie @ Mohd Zaidie Bin Adilai, Professor Madya TS Dr Mohd Hanafi Bin Ahmad Hijazi | ## 6.8 Responsibility Assignment Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for all design and governance recommendations, including architecture, security, and best practices. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for operationalizing the solution, ongoing administration, compliance, and user management. # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section defines the security architecture, network security group (NSG) rules, and encryption standards for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) in the Microsoft Fabric solution. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are critical security components that control inbound and outbound traffic to Azure resources. An NSG contains security rules that allow or deny network traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol. NSGs are typically associated with subnets or individual network interfaces to enforce granular security boundaries. Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following NSG rules for the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) VM and associated subnets to ensure secure connectivity to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft cloud services. ### 7.1.1 Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG VM | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |-----------------------------|----------|------------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and validating these NSG rules. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) shall implement and monitor these rules within their Azure environment. ## 7.2 Encryption Design Encryption is a fundamental security requirement for all data flows within the Microsoft Fabric architecture. Enfrasys Solutions prescribes the following encryption standards for UMS: ### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest All data stored in Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Azure SQL Database, and OneLake is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys and, where applicable, customer-managed keys (CMK) for enhanced control. This ensures that data residing on disk is protected from unauthorized access. ### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit Data transmitted between on-premises sources (via the OPDG VM), Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Azure SQL, and OneLake is encrypted in transit using industry-standard SSL/TLS protocols (HTTPS). This ensures confidentiality and integrity of data as it moves across networks. | No. | Policy | Decision | |-----|------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | Encryption at Rest | All data in Fabric, Power BI, Azure SQL, and OneLake is encrypted at rest with Microsoft-managed keys. | | 2 | Encryption in Transit | All data flows between components utilize SSL/TLS (HTTPS) for encryption in transit. | | 3 | NSG Enforcement | Outbound traffic is restricted to essential Microsoft endpoints only. | | 4 | Identity and Access Control | Multi-factor authentication (MFA) enforced at tenant level; Conditional Access for future review. | Enfrasys Solutions is accountable for the security architecture and encryption design. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for implementing, validating, and continuously monitoring encryption and access controls within their operational processes. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional Access enforcement for UMS Fabric. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\edeb3cb2-b1fc-4b7a-82d0-e06ccf12dc35\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies. ## 7.3 Security Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|---------| | NSG Rule Design | Define and document outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM and subnets | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete| | NSG Rule Implementation | Apply NSG rules to relevant Azure resources | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | | Encryption Architecture | Design encryption at rest and in transit for all data flows | Enfrasys Solutions | Complete| | Encryption Implementation | Configure encryption settings in Azure and Microsoft Fabric | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Pending | | Security Monitoring | Monitor NSG, encryption, and access policies for anomalies | Universiti Malaysia Sabah| Ongoing | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section defines the structured methodology for deploying and migrating the solution for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), including preparatory tasks, pilot, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup The pre-migration phase ensures all foundational elements are in place prior to deployment. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for planning, documentation, and initial environment setup. Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) is responsible for providing environment readiness and sign-off. | No. | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |-----|---------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys, UMS | Not-Started | | ### Timeline Table: Pre-Migration Phase | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D-7 | Design Document Review & Sign Off | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D-5 | Server Confirmation for Staging Area DB | UMS | Pending | | D-3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Enfrasys | Pending | | D-1 | On-Premises Data Gateway Installation | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | ## 8.2 Pilot The pilot phase validates the migration approach and platform readiness in a controlled environment. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the pilot deployment, with UMS providing test data and environment access. ### Pilot Deployment Steps 1. Enfrasys Solutions will configure pilot workloads within the Fabric environment. 2. UMS shall provide representative test data and confirm access to the Staging Area DB. 3. Enfrasys Solutions will install and configure the On-Premises Data Gateway for pilot data flows. 4. Enfrasys Solutions and UMS will jointly validate data ingestion and processing. 5. UMS will provide formal acceptance of pilot results. ### Timeline Table: Pilot Phase | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D0 | Pilot Workload Configuration | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+1 | Test Data Provision & Data Flow Validation| UMS, Enfrasys | Pending | | D+2 | Pilot Acceptance | UMS | Pending | ## 8.3 Production The production rollout encompasses the migration of all agreed workloads, system cutover, and validation. Enfrasys Solutions will execute migration and cutover activities; UMS is responsible for production environment readiness and final acceptance. ### Production Rollout Steps 1. Enfrasys Solutions will migrate production workloads to the Fabric environment. 2. UMS will ensure all required data sources are available and accessible. 3. Enfrasys Solutions will coordinate system cutover and switch production data flows. 4. Enfrasys Solutions and UMS will jointly validate production operations. 5. UMS will sign off on production system acceptance. ### Timeline Table: Production Phase | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|--------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D0 | Production Workload Migration | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+1 | Production Data Flow Cutover | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+2 | Production Validation & Acceptance | UMS, Enfrasys | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Post-migration activities ensure ongoing stability, knowledge transfer, and operational support. Enfrasys Solutions will provide post-go-live support and training; UMS is responsible for ongoing operations and issue escalation. ### Post-Migration/Support Activities 1. Enfrasys Solutions will monitor system stability and performance for an agreed support window. 2. Enfrasys Solutions will conduct knowledge transfer and operational handover sessions. 3. UMS will assume responsibility for daily operations and escalate any post-migration issues as needed. ### Timeline Table: Post-Migration/Support Phase | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|--------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | D+1 | Post-Go-Live Monitoring | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+2 | Knowledge Transfer & Handover | Enfrasys, UMS | Pending | | D+3 | Operational Ownership | UMS | Pending | # 9.0 Appendix This appendix provides reference materials, official documentation, and prerequisites for Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) to support the solution design and implementation. ## This is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

1. **Fabric-RG (Resource Group):**
   - Represents an Azure Resource Group named "Fabric-RG," which is a logical container for managing resources in Azure.
   - Contains the "UMS Fabric" component.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - Likely a service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG resource group.
   - Acts as the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to control access to the UMS Fabric.
   - Enforces policies such as multi-factor authentication, device compliance, or location-based access restrictions.

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an end-user or client accessing the UMS Fabric.
   - Access is mediated by the Conditional Access policies to ensure secure and compliant interaction.

**Data Flow:**
- The external user initiates a request to access the UMS Fabric.
- The request is intercepted by the Conditional Access layer, which evaluates the user's credentials and compliance with security policies.
- Upon successful validation, the user is granted access to the UMS Fabric hosted within the Fabric-RG resource group.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, either granting or denying access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed inbound access to Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric and OneLake**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric provides a suite of tools and services, represented by icons for various components such as data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence.
   - OneLake is highlighted as a central data lake within the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem, enabling unified data storage and management.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics pipeline within the **Microsoft Fabric** ecosystem, leveraging **Azure Data Factory**, **OneLake**, and **Power BI** for data ingestion, transformation, storage, and visualization.

1. **Data Ingestion**:
   - Data originates from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017** as a data warehouse.
   - The data is ingested into the pipeline using **Azure Data Factory**, which orchestrates the movement of data into the system.

2. **Bronze Layer**:
   - The ingested raw data is stored in **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**. This layer represents the initial landing zone for raw, unprocessed data.
   - The data is stored in **OneLake**, a unified data lake storage solution.

3. **Silver Layer**:
   - Data from the Bronze layer is transformed and refined into **XX_Silver_LK Tables**. This layer contains cleaned and enriched data, ready for further processing.
   - The transformation is managed by **Azure Data Factory**.

4. **Data Science and Gold Layer**:
   - The refined data from the Silver layer is further processed using **Data Science** tools for advanced analytics and machine learning.
   - The output is stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**, which represent the final, highly curated dataset.
   - **Identity_SM** is used for identity management or security-related processing in this stage.

5. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - The Gold layer data is connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake** for visualization and reporting.
   - Power BI provides interactive dashboards and reports for business insights.

### Key Components:
- **UMS Staging Area**: Source data repository.
- **Azure Data Factory**: Orchestration and ETL tool for data movement and transformation.
- **OneLake**: Centralized data lake for storage.
- **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: Raw data storage.
- **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Refined data storage.
- **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Curated data storage.
- **Identity_SM**: Security or identity management component.
- **Power BI**: Visualization and reporting tool.

### Data Flow:
1. Data is ingested from the UMS Staging Area into the Bronze layer via Data Factory.
2. Data is transformed and moved to the Silver layer.
3. Advanced analytics and machine learning are applied, and the results are stored in the Gold layer.
4. The Gold layer data is visualized in Power BI, connected through OneLake.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:
   - Represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database used as a staging area or data warehouse.
   - It stores and processes data before it is transferred to the next component.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:
   - Acts as a bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and the cloud-based UMS Fabric.
   - Facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.
   - Establishes an outbound secure connection to the cloud, ensuring data security and compliance.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Represents a cloud-based service or platform that consumes the data from the on-premises SQL Server.
   - Likely used for advanced analytics, reporting, or integration with other cloud services.

**Data Flow**:
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the On-Premise Data Gateway.
- The On-Premise Data Gateway securely transmits the data to the UMS Fabric using an outbound connection.
- The UMS Fabric processes or utilizes the data for further operations.

This architecture ensures secure and seamless integration between on-premises and cloud environments.

This image represents a **technical architecture diagram** illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source: XX_Silver_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Options**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - Automates the machine learning process.
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Conducts automated experiments to generate models.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: Scores the results of the AutoML experiments using a notebook.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - Allows for custom model development.
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Involves manual creation and training of models using notebooks.

4. **Processed Data: XX_Gold_LK Tables**:
   - Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) stored in **OneLake**.
   - Receives outputs from both AutoML and Manual Machine Learning processes.

5. **Identity_SM**:
   - A system or service module that interacts with the Gold tables for further processing or identity management.

6. **Power BI**:
   - Connects to the Gold tables via **OneLake**.
   - Enables visualization and reporting of the processed data.

### Connections:
- Data flows from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** to either **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** for processing.
- Outputs from these processes are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Power BI** accesses the Gold tables through **OneLake** for analytics and reporting.

This architecture demonstrates a flexible and scalable approach to data processing and machine learning within an Azure environment, leveraging both automated and manual methods for model development and scoring.

# SIRIM Analytics Deployment

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides an overview of the SIRIM Analytics Deployment project, outlines the purpose of this solution design document, and identifies the primary stakeholders.

## 2.1 Project Overview

The SIRIM Analytics Deployment project is designed to establish a comprehensive data workflow for SIRIM Berhad’s Human Resource (HR) use case. The project encompasses the end-to-end process from data ingestion, through transformation, to visualization, ensuring that HR data is efficiently processed and actionable insights are delivered. 

Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. Microsoft Fabric provides a seamless, scalable environment for orchestrating data workflows, enabling organizations to leverage advanced analytics and visualization capabilities.

SIRIM Berhad shall implement a data workflow aligned with Microsoft Fabric’s core services:
- **Fabric Data Factory**: A cloud-based data integration service that enables the creation, scheduling, and orchestration of data pipelines for ingesting and transforming data from diverse sources.
- **Fabric Synapse**: An integrated analytics service that combines big data and data warehousing capabilities, enabling advanced analytics and large-scale data processing.

The project aligns SIRIM Berhad’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities to support the HR analytics use case.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

This document serves as the formal Solution Design Document for the SIRIM Analytics Deployment. It provides the authoritative reference for implementation, operational governance, and ongoing management of the analytics solution. All technical decisions, design considerations, and implementation steps are detailed herein to ensure alignment between SIRIM Berhad and Enfrasys Solutions throughout the project lifecycle.

## 2.3 Document Audience

This Solution Design Document is intended for the following stakeholders:

| Stakeholder Group   | Role in Project                               |
|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| SIRIM IT            | Implementation, technical validation, review  |
| SIRIM HR            | Business requirements, data stewardship       |
| Enfrasys Solutions  | Solution design, implementation, support      |
| Project Sponsors    | Strategic oversight, project governance       |

Enfrasys Solutions is the primary author and owner of this document. SIRIM Berhad is responsible for reviewing, validating, and approving the design to ensure alignment with organizational objectives and compliance requirements.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric Analytics Overview

This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric Analytics and its integration with SIRIM Berhad’s data workflow, including supporting technologies for identity, access, and analytics.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric Platform

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform provided by Microsoft that unifies data engineering, data integration, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence on a single SaaS foundation. Microsoft Fabric brings together core services such as Data Factory, Synapse, and OneLake to deliver a seamless data experience.

- **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory is a cloud-based data integration service that allows organizations to create, schedule, and orchestrate data pipelines for ingesting, preparing, and transforming data from various sources.
- **Synapse**: Microsoft Fabric Synapse provides advanced analytics and big data processing capabilities. It enables organizations to analyze data using serverless or provisioned resources at scale, supporting both SQL and Spark workloads.
- **OneLake**: Microsoft OneLake is a unified data lake storage layer within Microsoft Fabric, enabling organizations to securely store, manage, and share data across analytics workloads. OneLake provides a single, logical data repository that integrates natively with Power BI and other Microsoft services.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM Berhad leverage Microsoft Fabric to streamline data ingestion, transformation, and analytics, ensuring scalability, security, and governance across the data lifecycle.

## 3.2 Microsoft 365 and Entra ID

- **Microsoft 365 (M365)**: Microsoft 365 is a suite of productivity and collaboration tools, including Office applications, Teams, SharePoint, and more. It provides a unified platform for communication, document management, and workflow automation, supporting integration with analytics and security services.
- **Microsoft Entra ID**: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management service. It delivers secure authentication, single sign-on, and conditional access controls for users and applications, ensuring robust identity governance and seamless integration with Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and other services.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM Berhad utilize Microsoft 365 and Entra ID as the foundation for identity, access, and collaboration, ensuring secure and efficient integration with the analytics platform.

## 3.3 Core Microsoft Technologies Utilized

The following table summarizes the core Microsoft technologies proposed for SIRIM Berhad’s analytics solution. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting the technology mapping; SIRIM Berhad shall validate and confirm alignment with organizational requirements.

| Name                      | Purpose                                                      | Key Features                                                                                   |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Microsoft Fabric          | Unified analytics platform                                   | Data integration, engineering, science, real-time analytics, BI, SaaS foundation              |
| Data Factory              | Data integration and orchestration                           | Pipeline creation, scheduling, transformation, connectors for multiple sources                 |
| Synapse                   | Advanced analytics and big data processing                   | SQL/Spark analytics, serverless/provisioned resources, data exploration                       |
| OneLake                   | Unified data lake storage                                    | Centralized storage, security, native integration with Power BI and Fabric services            |
| Power BI                  | Data visualization and business intelligence                 | Interactive dashboards, reports, semantic modeling, RLS, integration with OneLake              |
| Microsoft 365             | Productivity and collaboration suite                         | Office apps, Teams, SharePoint, workflow integration, security                                 |
| Microsoft Entra ID        | Identity and access management                               | Authentication, SSO, conditional access, integration with Microsoft services                   |

## 3.4 Data Workflow Architecture

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\09785e85-e592-4aba-bcb0-b465fe94ad6f\images\slide_016_img_004.png)
Figure 1: High-level data workflow architecture.

## 3.5 Design Considerations

| ID   | Description                                                                                      | Workload Type    |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from PostgreSQL.                                       | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from PostgreSQL.                      | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                 | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the bronze and silver Lakehouse will be performed using Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the gold Lakehouse. | Power BI         |
| DC08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.           | Power BI         |

| Validate Technology Mapping    | Review and confirm alignment with SIRIM Berhad’s requirements                | SIRIM Berhad        | Pending     |

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the hybrid network architecture for SIRIM Berhad, including connectivity, peering, and gateway design for secure and efficient access to Microsoft Fabric and related services.

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Microsoft Azure Virtual Network (VNET) is a fundamental building block for private network connectivity within Azure, enabling secure communication between Azure resources, on-premises networks, and external users. VNETs support segmentation, isolation, and integration with hybrid environments through VPNs and peering.

SIRIM Berhad shall implement a hybrid network architecture comprising the following components:

- **Site-to-Site (S2S) VPN**: Establishes secure connectivity between SIRIM Berhad’s on-premises infrastructure and Azure VNET 1 using a Virtual Network Gateway.
- **VNET Peering**: Enables seamless communication between VNET 1 (connected to on-premises) and VNET 2 (hosting Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD)), supporting cross-VNET data flows.
- **Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD)**: Provides secure remote access for external users, isolating them from core systems and enforcing access controls.
- **Fabric Private Endpoint**: Ensures that only resources within the internal network can access Fabric services, enhancing security and compliance.

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing the Azure network topology, while SIRIM Berhad shall provide detailed on-premises network information required for VPN configuration.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\09785e85-e592-4aba-bcb0-b465fe94ad6f\images\slide_012_img_003.png)
Figure 1: Hybrid network architecture with S2S VPN, VNET peering, and Fabric Private Endpoint.

## 4.2 Fabric Data Gateway

Microsoft Data Gateway is a secure bridge for transferring data between on-premises sources and Microsoft Fabric services, supporting scenarios such as Power BI, Azure Data Factory, and Logic Apps. The gateway enables hybrid data flows and maintains compliance by facilitating encrypted connections.

For SIRIM Berhad, the Data Gateway will be deployed on a dedicated Virtual Machine within the Azure environment. This VM serves as the intermediary between on-premises data sources and Fabric, ensuring secure and reliable data transfer.

Enfrasys Solutions shall design and provision the Data Gateway VM, while SIRIM Berhad is responsible for supplying relevant on-premises connectivity details and credentials.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\09785e85-e592-4aba-bcb0-b465fe94ad6f\images\slide_018_img_005.png)
Figure 2: Data Gateway VM bridging on-premises and Fabric services.

| 2  | VNET peering to enable cross-VNET communication and data flows               | Network/Platform   |
| 3  | WVD for external user isolation and secure access                            | Remote Access      |
| 4  | Dedicated Data Gateway VM for hybrid data integration                        | Data Integration   |
| 5  | Fabric Private Endpoint for internal-only access to Fabric resources         | Security/Platform  |

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments within Microsoft Fabric for SIRIM Berhad.

## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric: Formal Definition

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform provided by Microsoft that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence. It enables organizations to manage data workflows, enforce security, and empower users with analytics capabilities through a single SaaS solution, leveraging OneLake as a unified data lake.

## 5.2 Fabric Administrator Role

The Fabric Administrator is responsible for the overall management and governance of the Microsoft Fabric environment. This includes configuring workspaces, managing permissions, overseeing security policies, and ensuring compliance with organizational standards.

**Responsibilities of the Fabric Administrator:**
- Provision and manage Fabric workspaces.
- Assign and revoke user roles and permissions.
- Oversee data security and compliance, including row-level security (RLS).
- Monitor workspace activities and audit logs.
- Coordinate with Enfrasys Solutions for escalated support and platform enhancements.

**Assignment of Fabric Administrator Role:**
Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM Berhad assign the following personnel as Fabric Administrators, with SIRIM Berhad responsible for confirming and maintaining the assignments:

| No. | Workspace Role | Suggested Role | SIRIM PIC                |
|-----|---------------|----------------|--------------------------|
| 1   | Workspace Admin: add/remove users, manage roles and permissions, publish/edit/delete content | Fabric Admin/Data Engineer | En Wazir, Puan Solehah    |

## 5.3 Fabric-native Roles

Microsoft Fabric provides a set of native roles within each workspace to control access and capabilities. The following table outlines the key workspace-level roles and their permissions:

| Permission      | Admin | Contributor | Member | Viewer |
|-----------------|-------|-------------|--------|--------|
| View Content    | ✔     | ✔           | ✔      | ✔      |
| Edit Content    | ✔     | ✔           | ✔      |        |
| Delete Content  | ✔     | ✔ (own)     | ✔      |        |
| Share Content   | ✔     |             | ✔      |        |
| Manage Roles    | ✔     |             |        |        |
| Publish Content | ✔     | ✔           | ✔      |        |

**Role Definitions:**
- **Admin:** Full control over the workspace, including managing permissions and all content.
- **Contributor:** Can create, edit, and delete their own content but cannot manage roles or delete others' content.
- **Member:** Can create, edit, delete, and share content but cannot manage workspace roles or permissions.
- **Viewer:** Can view reports and dashboards but cannot edit or publish content.
- **Data Engineer:** Responsible for data pipelines, lakehouse management, and data transformation tasks.
- **Data Analyst:** Focuses on data modeling, report development, and implementing row-level security.

## 5.4 SIRIM Workspace Role and Access Mapping

Enfrasys Solutions recommends the following mapping of SIRIM Berhad personnel and groups to Microsoft Fabric workspace roles. SIRIM Berhad shall review, confirm, and maintain these assignments.

| No. | Workspace Role      | Suggested Role                  | SIRIM PIC                        |
|-----|--------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| 1   | Workspace Admin    | Fabric Admin/Data Engineer       | En Wazir, Puan Solehah           |
| 2   | Workspace Contributor | Data Engineer / Business User Admin | Aina, Khairul, Puan Solehah  |
| 3   | Workspace Viewer   | Data Analyst                     | Aina, Khairul, Puan Solehah      |
| 4   | Workspace Member   | Data Engineer                    | Aina, Khairul, Puan Solehah      |

## 5.5 Design Considerations for Role Assignment

| ID   | Description                                                                 | Workload Type      |
|------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| DC07 | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations in the Semantic Model | Power BI           |
| DC08 | Power BI Desktop connects to the Semantic Model via OneLake for visualization | Power BI           |

## 5.6 Access Decisions

| No. | Policy                                  | Decision                                      |
|-----|-----------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Assign workspace roles based on function and least privilege | Enfrasys recommends; SIRIM Berhad to confirm |
| 2   | Use Microsoft 365 groups for HR data access and RLS          | Enfrasys recommends; SIRIM Berhad to implement|

## 5.7 Administrator Capabilities

| Capability                | Description                                                                 |

| Workspace Provisioning    | Create and manage Fabric workspaces                                         |
| Role Management           | Assign and revoke user permissions                                          |
| Security Configuration    | Implement RLS and workspace-level security                                  |
| Audit and Monitoring      | Review activity logs and monitor compliance                                 |
| Integration Oversight     | Manage connections to data sources and external tools                       |

## 5.8 Row-Level Security and Data Modeling

SIRIM Berhad shall implement row-level security (RLS) within the Semantic Model to restrict data access based on user roles and Microsoft 365 group membership. This ensures sensitive data, such as HR information, is only accessible to authorized personnel.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric Design and Decision

This section outlines the design and decision process for implementing Microsoft Fabric within SIRIM Berhad’s analytics environment.

## 6.1 Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform provided by Microsoft, integrating data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS offering. It unifies data movement, storage, and analytics, supporting enterprise-scale workloads and governance.

The following table summarizes the key workflow design considerations for SIRIM Berhad’s Fabric deployment:

| ID    | Description                                                                                                  | Workload Type    |
|-------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01  | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from PostgreSQL.                                                   | Data Factory     |
| DC02  | The bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from PostgreSQL.                                  | Data Engineering |
| DC03  | The silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                             | Data Engineering |
| DC04  | The gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                            | Data Engineering |
| DC05  | Data cleansing and transformation for the bronze and silver Lakehouse will be performed using Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering |
| DC06  | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold).  | Data Factory     |
| DC07  | Data modeling and row-level security (RLS) configurations will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the gold Lakehouse. | Power BI         |
| DC08  | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                      | Power BI         |

## 6.2 Workflow Design Decisions

The following table details the final design decisions and rationale for each workflow consideration. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for leading the design and implementation, while SIRIM Berhad shall review and supply any missing information.

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                   | Decision                                                                                           |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DC01| Data ingestion from PostgreSQL will be orchestrated using Azure Data Factory pipelines.                           | Ensures scalable, managed, and auditable data ingestion.                                           |
| DC02| Raw data will be stored in the bronze Lakehouse within Fabric.                                                    | Preserves original data for traceability and reprocessing.                                          |
| DC03| Cleansed data will be stored in the silver Lakehouse after transformation.                                        | Supports data quality and readiness for downstream analytics.                                       |
| DC04| Transformed, business-ready data will be stored in the gold Lakehouse.                                            | Enables high-performance analytics and reporting.                                                   |
| DC05| Visual Query in SQL Analytics Endpoint will be used for data cleansing and transformation tasks.                  | Provides low-code, scalable data transformation capabilities.                                       |
| DC06| Dataflow Gen2 will manage data movement between Lakehouses.                                                       | Modernizes data orchestration with improved performance and monitoring.                             |
| DC07| Semantic Models will be built on the gold Lakehouse with RLS for secure, role-based access to data.               | Ensures security and compliance with SIRIM Berhad’s data governance policies.                      |
| DC08| Power BI Desktop will connect via OneLake to the Semantic Model for reporting and visualization.                  | Enables seamless, real-time analytics for business users.                                           |

## 6.5 Resource Organization Design

### 6.5.1 Management Groups

Enfrasys Solutions recommends structuring Azure Management Groups to logically separate production, development, and test environments. SIRIM Berhad shall review and finalize the hierarchy to align with internal governance and operational requirements.

### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging

#### Azure Resource Naming Convention

#### Acronyms

| PL      | Pipeline               |
| WS      | Workspace              |

## 6.6 Governance Consideration

### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines

| Resource         | Owner           | Budget         | Monitoring         |
|------------------|-----------------|---------------|--------------------|
| Fabric Workspace | SIRIM IT        | Defined by SIRIM| Azure Monitor, Cost Management |
| Data Gateway VM  | SIRIM IT        | Defined by SIRIM| Azure Monitor       |
| Data Pipelines   | SIRIM IT        | Defined by SIRIM| Azure Monitor       |

### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions

| Trigger                                   | Action                                         | Owner              |
|--------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| Unauthorized access attempt                | Alert, block access, investigate               | SIRIM IT           |
| Budget threshold exceeded                  | Notify, review, halt non-essential workloads   | SIRIM IT           |
| Public access detected on Fabric resource  | Immediate remediation, disable public endpoint | Enfrasys Solutions |

---

Enfrasys Solutions will lead the design, deployment, and governance configuration. SIRIM Berhad shall review, approve, and provide any additional information required for implementation.

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section outlines the security architecture for SIRIM Berhad, focusing on network segmentation, access control, and encryption for all data flows.

## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview

Microsoft Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are essential components that provide granular control over network traffic to and from Azure resources. NSGs allow administrators to define inbound and outbound security rules based on source, destination, port, and protocol, thereby enforcing a secure perimeter around critical workloads.

### Inbound NSG Rules

| Name                | Priority | Source      | Source Ports | Destination           | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|---------------------|----------|-------------|--------------|-----------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_Fabric_Inbound| 110      | Service Tag | 443          | VM Private IP Addresses| *                 | TCP      | Allow  |

### Outbound NSG Rules

| Name                 | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination  | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_Fabric_Outbound| 110      | VM Private IP Addresses| *            | Service Tag  | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Microsoft Azure provides robust encryption mechanisms to protect data both at rest and in transit. Encryption at rest ensures that stored data is protected using strong cryptographic algorithms, while encryption in transit secures data as it moves between users, services, and storage endpoints.

### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that SIRIM Berhad enable Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) for all data repositories, including Azure SQL Database, Azure Data Explorer, and Azure Storage accounts. This ensures that all data written to disk is automatically encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys or, optionally, customer-managed keys for enhanced compliance.

### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit

All communication between users, services, and Microsoft Fabric workloads shall be secured using industry-standard TLS 1.2 or higher. This includes access to the Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage. Enfrasys Solutions will define the encryption baseline, and SIRIM Berhad shall confirm that these controls meet their internal compliance requirements.

## 7.3 Conditional Access Design

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is a policy-based solution that enforces access controls on user identities, applications, and resources. Conditional Access enables SIRIM Berhad to require secure authentication methods such as Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), device compliance, and network location restrictions for accessing Microsoft Fabric, Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage.

### Access Design Decisions

| No. | Policy                                                                 | Decision                                                                                                          |
|-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | SIRIM users access Fabric directly, managed via Conditional Access      | Enfrasys Solutions to configure policies for secure login, MFA, and application-specific access                    |
| 2   | External users access Fabric indirectly via Windows Virtual Desktop     | Enfrasys Solutions to enforce NSG rules and isolate external sessions through WVD                                  |
| 3   | Conditional Access for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, SQL, Storage      | SIRIM Berhad to review and confirm policy scope and compliance with internal security requirements                 |

## 7.4 Security Task and Rollout Assignment

| Item                       | Activities                                                                                      | Action By           | Status  |
|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------|
| Define NSG Baseline        | Draft inbound and outbound NSG rules for Fabric and WVD networks                               | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Implement NSG Rules        | Apply approved NSG rules to relevant subnets and resources                                     | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Define Encryption Baseline | Document encryption standards for data at rest and in transit                                  | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Compliance Review          | Review and confirm encryption and access control compliance requirements                        | SIRIM Berhad        | Pending |
| Configure Conditional Access| Deploy Conditional Access policies for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, SQL, and Storage         | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| User Acceptance            | Validate and test access scenarios for both SIRIM and external users                           | SIRIM Berhad        | Pending |

## 7.5 Security Architecture Diagram

![](images/architecture/security_network_topology.png)
Figure 1: Security network topology illustrating NSG enforcement and access flows.

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the structured approach for deployment and migration, including preparatory tasks, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support for SIRIM Berhad.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

All preparatory tasks required prior to migration are detailed below, with clear assignment of responsibilities and status tracking.

| Task                                                    | Owner           | Due Date         | Status       |
|---------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|------------------|--------------|
| Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by SIRIM         | Enfrasys, SIRIM | 17-June-2025     | Not-Started  |
| Confirm on the table to be ingested                     | SIRIM           | 13-June-2025     | Not-Started  |
| Create an Azure VM to establish Data Gateway            | Enfrasys        | 18-June-2025     | Not-Started  |
| Conduct session to understand current HR data workflow  | SIRIM, Enfrasys | 14-June-2025     | Not-Started  |
| Confirm query list for ingestion with Encik Wazir       | SIRIM           | 18-June-2025     | Not-Started  |

## 8.2 Pilot

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment to validate the migration strategy and ensure system compatibility with SIRIM Berhad’s requirements.

### Pilot Deployment Steps

1. Deploy Azure VM and configure Data Gateway for a subset of identified tables.
2. Ingest sample data and validate data integrity.
3. Conduct end-to-end workflow testing with SIRIM Berhad’s HR data.
4. Gather feedback from SIRIM Berhad stakeholders.
5. Document issues and remediation actions.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Successful data ingestion with no data loss or corruption.
- All critical queries execute as expected.
- Stakeholder sign-off on pilot results.

## 8.3 Production

The production deployment will follow a structured timeline to minimize risk and ensure business continuity for SIRIM Berhad.

| Milestone                                  | Date           | Owner           | Status       |
|---------------------------------------------|----------------|-----------------|--------------|
| Pre-Migration (D-7): Final readiness review | 18-June-2025   | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending      |
| Cutover (D0): Execute production migration  | 25-June-2025   | Enfrasys        | Pending      |
| Post-Migration (D+1): Validation & support  | 26-June-2025   | Enfrasys, SIRIM | Pending      |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Post-migration activities ensure a smooth transition and knowledge transfer to SIRIM Berhad’s operational teams.

| Item                               | Activities                                              | Action By         | Status       |
|------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------------|
| Post-migration validation          | Verify data integrity and system performance           | Enfrasys, SIRIM   | Pending      |
| Issue resolution                   | Address any defects or incidents post-migration        | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| Knowledge transfer                 | Conduct training and handover sessions                 | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| Documentation handover             | Deliver all updated technical and process documents    | Enfrasys          | Pending      |
| Transition to BAU support          | SIRIM assumes operational responsibility               | SIRIM             | Pending      |
