slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review

# 2.0 Executive Summary

This section provides an overview of the UMS Power BI & Fabric project, the purpose of this Solution Design Document, and the intended audience for review and sign-off.

## 2.1 Project Overview

UMS has engaged Enfrasys Solutions to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow solution leveraging Microsoft Fabric. The project scope encompasses the establishment of a secure and scalable Fabric workspace, the development of end-to-end ETL pipelines, the execution of machine learning processes, and the integration of Power BI for advanced business analytics. The primary objectives are to align UMS’s cloud adoption strategy with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities, specifically utilizing Fabric Data Factory for data integration and orchestration, Fabric Synapse for advanced analytics and big data processing, and Data Science features for machine learning applications.

## 2.2 Document Purpose

This Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for the architecture, design decisions, and implementation approach for the UMS Power BI & Fabric project. It defines the technical blueprint, ensures alignment with UMS’s business requirements, and establishes the foundation for subsequent implementation, testing, and operational phases. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for drafting and maintaining this document, while UMS is responsible for reviewing and providing formal sign-off to confirm alignment with organizational objectives.

## 2.3 Document Audience

The intended audience for this document includes:

- UMS IT Department: Responsible for technical review, operational readiness, and ongoing management.
- UMS Business Stakeholders: Accountable for validating that the solution meets business objectives and analytics requirements.
- Enfrasys Solutions Project Team: Responsible for solution design, implementation, and technical delivery.
- UMS Project Sponsors: Responsible for executive oversight and project governance.

Enfrasys Solutions shall facilitate document walkthroughs and address all technical queries. UMS shall review, provide feedback, and formally approve the Solution Design Document prior to project implementation.

# 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview

This section provides an overview of the core Microsoft technologies—Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID—recommended for UMS’s data platform modernization initiative.

## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric

Microsoft Fabric is an integrated, end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence under a single SaaS solution. Fabric brings together several core services, including Data Factory, Synapse, OneLake, and Data Science, to enable organizations to manage the entire data lifecycle from ingestion to insight.

- **Data Factory**: Microsoft Fabric Data Factory is a cloud-based data integration service that allows users to create, schedule, and orchestrate data pipelines. It supports data ingestion, transformation, and movement across various sources, enabling UMS to automate ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes efficiently.
- **Synapse**: Fabric Synapse provides advanced analytics capabilities, including big data processing, data warehousing, and real-time analytics. It empowers UMS to analyze large-scale datasets and extract actionable insights for business decision-making.
- **OneLake**: OneLake is a unified data lake storage system within Fabric that centralizes all organizational data in a secure, scalable, and compliant manner. It simplifies data management and access for analytics and machine learning workloads.
- **Data Science**: The Data Science service in Fabric offers integrated machine learning tools and AutoML capabilities, enabling UMS to build, train, and deploy predictive models directly within the platform.

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS leverage Microsoft Fabric to establish a centralized data workflow, supporting seamless data ingestion, transformation, advanced analytics, and machine learning processes.

## 3.2 Power BI

Power BI is Microsoft’s industry-leading business intelligence (BI) platform that enables organizations to visualize data, build interactive reports, and share insights across the enterprise. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric, allowing users to consume data from OneLake, analyze results from Synapse, and visualize machine learning outputs from Data Science modules.

Key capabilities of Power BI include:
- Real-time data visualization and dashboarding
- Self-service analytics for business users
- Secure sharing and collaboration of reports and datasets
- Integration with Microsoft Fabric for unified data access

For UMS, Enfrasys Solutions recommends integrating Power BI within the Fabric workspace to provide business-aligned analytics, enabling stakeholders to make data-driven decisions based on timely, accurate, and actionable insights.

## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID

Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft’s cloud-based identity and access management (IAM) service. It provides secure authentication, single sign-on (SSO), and role-based access control (RBAC) for users and applications across Microsoft cloud services.

In the context of UMS’s solution, Entra ID will:
- Authenticate users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI services
- Manage user identities and enforce security policies
- Enable granular access controls to datasets, reports, and machine learning assets within the Fabric workspace

Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS utilize Microsoft Entra ID as the foundation for secure authentication and access management, ensuring compliance and safeguarding sensitive business data throughout the analytics lifecycle.

# 4.0 Network Design and Decision

This section outlines the network architecture for UMS, including connectivity between on-premises infrastructure and Microsoft Fabric, and the secure design and deployment of the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG).

## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview

Enfrasys Solutions recommends a hybrid network topology for UMS, ensuring secure and reliable data flows between on-premises systems and Microsoft Fabric in Azure. The design leverages outbound-only connections from the on-premises environment, minimizing the attack surface and adhering to Azure networking best practices.

The architecture enforces access controls using Microsoft Entra Conditional Access, ensuring only authorized users and systems can interact with the UMS Fabric workloads. All data flows between on-premises SQL Server, the OPDG, and Microsoft Fabric are encrypted and routed through secure outbound connections.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\f3ff645c-da21-4475-b630-8b749796539e\images\slide_006_img_002.png)
Figure 1: Authentication and secure access flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\f3ff645c-da21-4475-b630-8b749796539e\images\slide_011_img_004.png)
Figure 2: Data flow architecture between on-premises SQL Server, OPDG, and UMS Fabric.

## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway

### 4.2.1 Technology Definition

The Microsoft On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) is a secure bridge that facilitates data transfer between on-premises data sources (such as SQL Server) and Microsoft cloud services, including Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure Logic Apps. The OPDG enables organizations to keep their data sources on-premises while securely leveraging cloud analytics and reporting capabilities. It operates by establishing outbound encrypted connections to Microsoft cloud endpoints, eliminating the need for inbound firewall rules.

### 4.2.2 OPDG Design for UMS

Enfrasys Solutions recommends deploying the OPDG agent on a dedicated virtual machine within UMS’s on-premises environment. This gateway will serve as the secure conduit for data synchronization between the UMS SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric workloads in Azure.

#### VM Specification for OPDG

| Component        | Specification                                      |
|------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit)|
| .NET Framework   | Version 4.8                                        |
| vCPU             | 8 cores                                            |
| RAM              | Minimum 8 GB                                       |

#### Deployment Responsibilities

| Item                           | Activities                                                            | Action By           | Status  |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------|
| OPDG VM Provisioning           | Provision VM meeting specification; OS and network configuration      | UMS                 | Pending |
| .NET Framework Installation    | Install .NET Framework 4.8 on designated VM                           | UMS                 | Pending |
| OPDG Agent Deployment          | Install and configure OPDG agent, register with Microsoft Fabric      | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |
| Network Rule Configuration     | Implement outbound NSG rules as per design                            | UMS                 | Pending |
| Connectivity Validation        | Test secure connectivity from OPDG to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI   | Enfrasys Solutions  | Pending |

#### Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG

| Name                        | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                          | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|-----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services   | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication   | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                      | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

### 4.2.3 Design Decisions

| No. | Design Decision                                                                                                  | Decision                                                                                 |
|-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 1   | OPDG to be deployed on a dedicated VM meeting Microsoft’s recommended specifications.                            | Approved; UMS to provision VM, Enfrasys to deploy and configure OPDG.                    |
| 2   | Outbound-only connectivity from on-premises to Microsoft Fabric and related cloud endpoints.                     | Approved; all inbound ports remain closed.                                               |
| 3   | NSG rules to allow only required outbound HTTPS/HTTP traffic to Microsoft service domains.                       | Approved as per table above.                                                             |
| 4   | Conditional Access policies to govern user authentication to Microsoft Fabric.                                   | Enforced via Entra Conditional Access as illustrated in the network diagrams.            |

## 4.3 Summary of Network Design Considerations

| ID  | Description                                                                                                 | Workload Type           |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| N1  | Hybrid connectivity with secure, outbound-only data flows between on-premises and Azure                     | Data Analytics, BI      |
| N2  | Deployment of OPDG as a bridge for SQL Server to Microsoft Fabric integration                               | Data Gateway            |
| N3  | Enforcement of Conditional Access for all inbound user authentication to Microsoft Fabric                   | Security, Access Control|
| N4  | Implementation of granular NSG outbound rules to restrict traffic to only required Microsoft endpoints      | Security, Networking    |

# 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI

This section defines the roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for UMS personnel in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments.

## 5.1 Fabric Administrator Role

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that brings together data movement, data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a unified SaaS experience. The Fabric Administrator role is a privileged role that provides comprehensive management capabilities across the Fabric environment.

### Responsibilities and Permissions

Fabric Administrators are responsible for the overall governance, configuration, and security of the Microsoft Fabric platform. Their permissions typically include:

| Capability                        | Description                                                                                      |
|------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Tenant-level settings management   | Configure and enforce global policies across all workspaces and workloads.                       |
| User and group management          | Add, remove, and manage user access and security groups within Fabric.                           |
| Workspace lifecycle management     | Create, update, and delete workspaces; assign workspace roles.                                   |
| Resource monitoring and auditing   | View usage metrics, audit logs, and monitor compliance across the Fabric environment.             |
| Security and compliance oversight  | Configure data protection, privacy, and compliance features.                                     |
| Integration and connectivity       | Manage connections to external data sources and integration with other Microsoft services.        |

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for defining the Fabric Administrator role and its associated permissions. UMS shall assign qualified personnel to this role and maintain user management activities.

# 6.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Design and Decision

This section outlines the solution design for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI within UMS, covering workflow, access, VM sizing, resource organization, and governance.

## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations

Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data integration, data warehousing, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence (BI) on a single SaaS foundation. Power BI is a business analytics service by Microsoft that provides interactive visualizations and business intelligence capabilities with an interface simple enough for end users to create their own reports and dashboards.

The following table summarizes the principal design considerations for both main and AutoML data workflows within UMS’s Microsoft Fabric implementation:

| ID   | Description                                                                                                                    | Workload Type    |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|
| DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server.                                                           | Data Factory     |
| DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server.                                          | Data Engineering |
| DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse.                                               | Data Engineering |
| DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse.                                              | Data Engineering |
| DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook and Visual Query.   | Data Engineering |
| DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., bronze to silver, silver to gold).                           | Data Factory     |
| DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse. | Data Science     |
| DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse.                                   | Power BI         |
| DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                        | Power BI         |
| DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation.                  | Data Engineering |
| DE02 | The project will primarily adopt Manual Machine Learning using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python).                           | Data Science     |
| DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment.                           | Data Science     |
| DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry.                                                                | Data Science     |
| DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data, with results stored in Gold Lakehouse.           | Data Engineering |
| DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results.                                | Data Engineering |
| DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented.        | Power BI         |
| DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization.                                        | Power BI         |

| 1   | Technology Stack Selection                                                                                         | Microsoft Fabric and Power BI selected for unified analytics, governed data flows, and scalable BI.         |
| 2   | Data Ingestion and Transformation                                                                                  | Azure Data Factory orchestrates ingestion from SQL Server to Bronze, Silver, and Gold Lakehouses.           |

Enfrasys Solutions recommends UMS adopt a tiered management group structure to separate environments (e.g., Production, Development, Staging) under a single root management group for centralized policy and compliance enforcement. ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines

| Cost Management Discipline | Azure Tool         | Owner             |
|---------------------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| Budgeting                 | Azure Cost Management + Billing | UMS               |
| Resource Tagging          | Azure Policy       | UMS               |
| Spend Analysis            | Power BI, Azure Cost Analysis | UMS               |
| Recommendations/Optimization | Azure Advisor      | Enfrasys Solutions |
| Policy/Compliance         | Azure Policy       | Enfrasys Solutions |

### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions

| Trigger                                    | Action                                           | Owner             |
|---------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| Resource exceeds budget                     | Notify stakeholders, review resource allocation  | UMS               |
| Non-compliant resource detected             | Remediate per Azure Policy, escalate if repeated | UMS               |
| Unauthorized access attempt                 | Investigate, enforce MFA, review access policies | UMS               |
| Unapproved naming/tagging                   | Notify resource owner, enforce correction        | UMS               |
| Policy violation (e.g., missing tags)       | Automated remediation, report to governance team | Enfrasys Solutions |

---

Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the overall design, technology recommendations, and governance framework. UMS is responsible for operationalizing, enforcing, and maintaining compliance with all resource, access, and governance policies.

# 7.0 Security Design and Decision

This section defines the security architecture for the UMS Fabric deployment, including network security, encryption, and access controls.

## 7.1 Azure Network Security Group (NSG) Overview

Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are essential security components that allow or deny network traffic to Azure resources within a virtual network. NSGs contain rules that filter inbound and outbound traffic based on source/destination IP address, port, and protocol.

### Outbound NSG Rules for On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG)

The following outbound rules are required to ensure secure connectivity from the OPDG VM to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules are designed to allow only necessary traffic, following Azure security best practices.

| Name                       | Priority | Source                | Source Ports | Destination                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access |
|----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------|
| Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services  | 130      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Authentication  | 140      | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com                                                                             | 443               | TCP      | Allow  |
| Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test | 160    | VM Private IP Addresses | *            | *.msftncsi.com                                                                                                                                                                                              | 80                | TCP      | Allow  |

> Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and validation of these NSG rules. UMS shall implement and maintain these rules within the designated Azure resource group.

## 7.2 Encryption Design

Encryption is a foundational security requirement for all data flows within the UMS Fabric solution. Enfrasys Solutions recommends a defense-in-depth approach, utilizing both encryption at rest and in transit.

### 7.2.1 Encryption at Rest

All data stored within Microsoft Fabric components—including OneLake, SQL Server, and Power BI datasets—is encrypted at rest using Microsoft-managed keys by default. Customers may optionally implement customer-managed keys (CMK) for enhanced control. Data stored on the On-Premises Data Gateway VM must utilize BitLocker or equivalent disk encryption.

| Component                | Encryption at Rest Mechanism                          | Responsibility      |
|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
| OneLake                  | Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE)               | Microsoft           |
| SQL Server (PaaS)        | Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)                    | Microsoft           |
| Power BI                 | Azure Storage Encryption                             | Microsoft           |
| OPDG VM (on-premises)    | BitLocker or equivalent                              | UMS                 |

### 7.2.2 Encryption in Transit

All data in transit between UMS users, the On-Premises Data Gateway, and Microsoft Fabric services is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher. This includes:

- HTTPS (TCP 443) for all communications between OPDG and Microsoft cloud endpoints.
- Enforced TLS for Power BI, SQL Database, and OneLake connections.
- MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) enforced at the tenant level for all UMS user access to Microsoft Fabric resources.

| Data Flow                                   | Encryption in Transit | Responsibility      |
|---------------------------------------------|----------------------|---------------------|
| OPDG ↔ Microsoft Fabric/Power BI            | TLS 1.2+ (HTTPS)     | Enfrasys (design), UMS (on-premises config) |
| UMS Users ↔ Microsoft Fabric/Power BI       | TLS 1.2+ (HTTPS), MFA| Enfrasys (design), UMS (user MFA enforcement) |
| Internal Azure Service Communications       | Encrypted by default | Microsoft           |

> Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and validation of encryption controls. UMS is responsible for implementing and maintaining encryption on all on-premises assets, including the Data Gateway VM.

## 7.3 Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication

Microsoft Entra Conditional Access is a policy-based approach to secure access, evaluating user, device, and environmental signals before granting access to resources. Due to current licensing constraints, UMS is not eligible for Conditional Access at this time. Instead, MFA is enforced at the tenant level to ensure secure user authentication.

| No. | Policy                                                                 | Decision                                      |
|-----|------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| 1   | Configure Conditional Access for Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, Azure Storage | Not implemented (licensing constraints)       |
| 2   | Include UMS users in Conditional Access policy                          | Not implemented (licensing constraints)       |
| 3   | Enforce MFA for all UMS users                                          | Implemented                                   |
| 4   | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric environment                       | Allowed via MFA                               |

> Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the design and recommendation of access policies. UMS is responsible for enforcing MFA and monitoring user compliance.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\f3ff645c-da21-4475-b630-8b749796539e\images\slide_006_img_002.png)
Figure 1: Conditional Access and authentication flow for Microsoft Fabric.

![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\f3ff645c-da21-4475-b630-8b749796539e\images\slide_006_img_001.png)
Figure 2: Secure access workflow with Conditional Access enforcement.

## 7.4 Security Task Assignment and Rollout

| Item               | Activities                                                                 | Action By          | Status      |
|--------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|-------------|
| NSG Rule Design    | Define and validate outbound NSG rules for OPDG                           | Enfrasys Solutions | Completed   |
| NSG Rule Deployment| Implement NSG rules in Azure                                              | UMS                | Pending     |
| Encryption Design  | Define encryption at rest and in transit standards                        | Enfrasys Solutions | Completed   |
| On-prem Encryption | Enable BitLocker on OPDG VM                                               | UMS                | Pending     |
| MFA Enforcement    | Enforce MFA at tenant level                                               | UMS                | Completed   |
| Security Review    | Review and validate security implementation                               | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending     |

# 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach

This section outlines the structured deployment and migration approach for the UMS project, detailing preparatory actions, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support with clear responsibility assignments.

## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup

All preparatory actions must be completed prior to pilot and production deployment. The following table assigns ownership and tracks status for each activity.

| No. | Activities                                                        | Planned Start     | Action By      | Status       | Remarks |
|-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------------|--------------|---------|
| 1   | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off          | Fri, 7-Nov-25     | Enfrasys<br>UMS| Not-Started  |         |
| 2   | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS            | Not-Started  |         |
| 3   | Fabric Environment Setup                                           | Wed, 12-Nov-25    | Enfrasys       | Not-Started  |         |
| 4   | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys<br>UMS| Not-Started  |         |

## 8.2 Pilot

The pilot deployment phase is critical for validating the migration approach and identifying potential issues before full-scale rollout. Enfrasys Solutions will lead the pilot deployment, with UMS providing necessary access and validation.

**Pilot Deployment Steps and Responsibilities:**

1. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure the Microsoft Fabric environment in a controlled pilot workspace.
2. UMS shall provide access to the staging area database and validate test data ingestion.
3. Enfrasys Solutions shall install and configure the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) on the designated VM.
4. UMS shall participate in user acceptance testing (UAT) and provide feedback on the pilot outcomes.
5. Enfrasys Solutions shall document pilot results and recommend adjustments for production deployment.

| Item | Activities                                              | Action By        | Status      |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------|
| 1    | Configure pilot Fabric workspace                       | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 2    | Provide staging area DB access                         | UMS              | Pending     |
| 3    | Install & configure OPDG in pilot environment          | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 4    | Conduct UAT and feedback session                       | UMS              | Pending     |
| 5    | Document pilot results and refine deployment plan      | Enfrasys         | Pending     |

## 8.3 Production

The production deployment will follow a phased approach, with clear timelines and responsibilities assigned for each phase.

### D-7: Pre-Production

- Enfrasys Solutions shall finalize configuration of the production Microsoft Fabric environment.
- UMS shall confirm readiness of all data sources and user accounts.
- Enfrasys Solutions shall perform connectivity and security validation.

### D0: Cutover

- Enfrasys Solutions shall initiate migration of data pipelines and activate the production On-Premises Data Gateway.
- UMS shall validate initial data loads and confirm business continuity.
- Enfrasys Solutions shall provide hypercare support during cutover.

### D+1: Post-Cutover

- UMS shall perform end-user validation and sign-off.
- Enfrasys Solutions shall monitor system performance and resolve any post-migration issues.

| Item | Activities                                              | Action By        | Status      |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------|
| 1    | Finalize production Fabric environment configuration   | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 2    | Confirm data source and user account readiness         | UMS              | Pending     |
| 3    | Validate connectivity and security                     | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 4    | Initiate data pipeline migration and OPDG activation   | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 5    | Validate initial data loads                            | UMS              | Pending     |
| 6    | Provide hypercare support during cutover               | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 7    | End-user validation and sign-off                       | UMS              | Pending     |
| 8    | Monitor performance and resolve issues                 | Enfrasys         | Pending     |

## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support

Post-migration support ensures a smooth transition to steady-state operations and knowledge transfer to UMS personnel.

**Support and Handover Activities:**

1. Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration monitoring and incident response for a defined stabilization period.
2. UMS shall report any operational issues encountered during the stabilization period.
3. Enfrasys Solutions shall conduct a formal handover session, including documentation and knowledge transfer.
4. UMS shall acknowledge receipt of documentation and confirm operational readiness.

| Item | Activities                                              | Action By        | Status      |
|------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------|
| 1    | Post-migration monitoring and incident response        | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 2    | Issue reporting during stabilization                   | UMS              | Pending     |
| 3    | Conduct handover and knowledge transfer session        | Enfrasys         | Pending     |
| 4    | Acknowledge documentation and operational readiness    | UMS              | Pending     |

This is a technical architecture diagram.

**Description:**

1. **Fabric-RG (Resource Group):**
   - Represents an Azure Resource Group named "Fabric-RG," which is a logical container for managing resources in Azure.
   - Contains the "UMS Fabric" component.

2. **UMS Fabric:**
   - Likely a service or application hosted within the Fabric-RG.
   - Acts as the core system or platform that external users interact with.

3. **Conditional Access:**
   - A security mechanism applied to manage and control access to the UMS Fabric.
   - Ensures that only authorized users can access the system based on predefined policies (e.g., user identity, device compliance, location).

4. **External User:**
   - Represents an external user or client attempting to access the UMS Fabric.
   - Access is mediated through the Conditional Access layer to enforce security policies.

5. **Data Flow:**
   - The arrow indicates a unidirectional flow of access or interaction from the external user to the UMS Fabric.
   - The Conditional Access layer acts as a gatekeeper, validating the user's credentials and compliance before granting access to the UMS Fabric.

This diagram illustrates a secure access workflow where external users interact with a system hosted in Azure, with Conditional Access enforcing security policies.

This image is a technical architecture diagram illustrating the authentication and access control flow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra Conditional Access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**:  
   - The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**:  
   - The authentication request is evaluated against Entra Conditional Access policies. This involves three key stages:
     - **Signal**: Signals such as user identity, group membership, and network location are collected. Examples include:
       - Users and groups.
       - Network location.
     - **Decision**: Based on the signals, a decision is made regarding access.
     - **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

3. **Inbound Access to Microsoft Fabric**:  
   - If access is granted, the user is allowed to interact with Microsoft Fabric.

4. **Microsoft Fabric Components**:  
   - Microsoft Fabric includes various components for data and analytics, such as:
     - Data Factory.
     - Synapse Data Engineering.
     - Synapse Data Science.
     - Synapse Data Warehousing.
     - Synapse Real-Time Analytics.
     - Power BI.
   - **OneLake**: A unified data lake is also part of the ecosystem, serving as a central repository for data storage.

This diagram effectively demonstrates the integration of conditional access policies with Microsoft Fabric to ensure secure and controlled access to its components.

This is a technical architecture diagram.

### Description:

1. **Data Source**:
   - The process begins with data ingestion from the **UMS Staging Area**, which is hosted on **Microsoft SQL Server 2017**. This serves as the data warehouse for raw data.

2. **Data Ingestion**:
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used to ingest data from the staging area into the system. This is the first step in the data pipeline.

3. **UMS Fabric**:
   - The data flows into the **UMS Fabric**, which is the central framework for data processing and transformation. Within the UMS Fabric:
     - **XX_Bronze_LK Tables**: This layer stores raw ingested data in its initial form.
     - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Data is transformed and cleaned in this layer, making it more structured and ready for analysis.
     - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: This layer contains the final, highly curated data, optimized for reporting and analytics.

4. **Data Transformation**:
   - **Azure Data Factory** is used at multiple stages to orchestrate and manage data movement and transformation between the Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers.
   - **Data Science** tools are integrated into the pipeline, enabling advanced analytics and machine learning processes on the Silver layer data.

5. **Identity Management**:
   - The **Identity_SM** component is used for managing identity and security, ensuring that data access is controlled and compliant with organizational policies.

6. **Data Storage**:
   - The processed data is stored in **OneLake**, a unified data lake that supports seamless integration with downstream tools.

7. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - The final curated data in the Gold layer is connected to **Power BI** via **OneLake** for visualization, reporting, and business intelligence.

### Data Flow:
- Data flows sequentially from the **UMS Staging Area** → **Bronze Layer** → **Silver Layer** → **Gold Layer**.
- At each stage, **Azure Data Factory** orchestrates the movement and transformation of data.
- **Data Science** and **Identity_SM** components enhance the pipeline with analytics and security.
- The final output is visualized in **Power BI**, leveraging the integration with **OneLake**.

This image is a technical architecture diagram.

The diagram illustrates a data flow between three main components:

1. **UMS - Staging_Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 - Data Warehouse**:  
   - This represents an on-premises SQL Server 2017 database acting as a staging area or data warehouse. It stores and processes data for further use.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**:  
   - The OPDG serves as a secure bridge between the on-premises SQL Server and external cloud services.  
   - It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports to be opened in the on-premises network.  
   - The connection is outbound and secure, ensuring data integrity and security during transmission.

3. **UMS Fabric**:  
   - This is likely a cloud-based service or platform that consumes or processes the data from the on-premises SQL Server.  
   - The UMS Fabric interacts with the OPDG to retrieve data securely.

**Data Flow**:  
- Data flows from the SQL Server database to the OPDG via a secure connection.  
- The OPDG then establishes an outbound secure connection to the UMS Fabric, enabling data transfer without exposing the on-premises environment to inbound traffic.  
- The bidirectional arrows indicate that data can flow both ways, allowing for data retrieval and updates as needed.

This image represents a technical architecture diagram illustrating a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure-based ecosystem.

### Components and Data Flow:
1. **UMS Fabric**:
   - Acts as the overarching framework for the workflow, encompassing all components.

2. **Data Source**:
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Represents the intermediate data layer (Silver tables) stored in **OneLake**. This serves as the input for machine learning processes.

3. **Machine Learning Workflows**:
   - **AutoML**:
     - **AutoML Experiment**: Automatically generates machine learning models using the data from the Silver tables.
     - **Result Scoring (Using Notebook)**: The results of the AutoML experiment are scored and evaluated using a notebook.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - **Modeling (Using Notebook)**: Allows for custom machine learning model development using notebooks, providing flexibility for advanced use cases.

4. **Processed Data Output**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Represents the refined data layer (Gold tables) where the results of the machine learning processes are stored.
   - **Identity_SM**: A specific component or service that interacts with the Gold tables, possibly for identity management or further processing.

5. **Visualization and Reporting**:
   - **Power BI**:
     - Connected to the Gold tables via **OneLake**, enabling data visualization and reporting for business insights.

### Connections:
- Data flows from **XX_Silver_LK Tables** to either the **AutoML** or **Manual Machine Learning** processes.
- The outputs from these processes are stored in **XX_Gold_LK Tables**.
- **Power BI** accesses the Gold tables through **OneLake** for visualization purposes.

This architecture demonstrates a streamlined approach to data processing, machine learning, and business intelligence within an Azure environment.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.

# UMS Power BI & Fabric Design Review # 2.0 Executive Summary This section provides a concise overview of the UMS Power BI & Microsoft Fabric solution, its purpose, and the intended audience for the design document. ## 2.1 Project Overview Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single SaaS solution. It provides services such as Data Factory (for data integration and orchestration), Synapse (for advanced analytics and big data processing), and Data Science (for machine learning applications). UMS is undertaking this project to design and implement a comprehensive data workflow leveraging Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. The scope includes establishing a Fabric workspace, developing end-to-end ETL pipelines, executing machine learning processes, and integrating Power BI for business-aligned analytics. The objective is to modernize UMS’s data infrastructure, streamline data ingestion, transformation, and visualization, and align cloud adoption with Microsoft Fabric’s capabilities. ## 2.2 Document Purpose The Solution Design Document serves as the authoritative reference for validating the proposed architecture, guiding implementation, and ensuring governance of the UMS Power BI & Fabric solution. It documents design decisions, technical specifications, and operational procedures to facilitate successful delivery and ongoing management. This document is intended for use during design review, implementation planning, and as a baseline for future enhancements. ## 2.3 Document Audience The intended readers of this Solution Design Document are: - UMS IT department - UMS business stakeholders - Enfrasys Solutions delivery and architecture teams Responsibility for review and sign-off of this document is jointly assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. Both parties must validate the design, confirm alignment with business objectives, and authorize implementation. # 3.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Overview This section provides an overview of Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Microsoft Entra ID, outlining their roles within the UMS solution and the management responsibilities assigned to Enfrasys Solutions and UMS. ## 3.1 Microsoft Fabric: Definition and Core Services Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data science, data integration, and business intelligence within a single SaaS environment. It enables organizations to ingest, transform, analyze, and visualize data using a cohesive set of services, all underpinned by OneLake—a unified data lake architecture. Fabric’s core services include: - **Data Factory**: Provides cloud-scale data integration and orchestration capabilities, supporting ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines for ingesting and processing data from diverse sources. - **Synapse**: Delivers advanced analytics and big data processing, offering tools for data warehousing, real-time analytics, and data exploration. - **Data Science**: Facilitates machine learning and AI workflows, enabling both automated (AutoML) and manual modeling, experimentation, and deployment. Within the UMS solution, Microsoft Fabric serves as the central platform for end-to-end data workflow, encompassing data ingestion from SQL Server, transformation through Bronze/Silver/Gold tables, machine learning processes, and integration with Power BI for business analytics. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for designing and deploying the Fabric workspace, configuring core services, and establishing the ETL pipeline. UMS shall manage ongoing data operations, user access, and workspace governance. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: UMS Data Workflow Architecture in Microsoft Fabric. ## 3.2 Power BI: Overview and Integration Power BI is Microsoft’s enterprise-grade business intelligence platform, designed to enable users to visualize, analyze, and share data insights across the organization. It provides interactive dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics, supporting data-driven decision-making. Power BI integrates natively with Microsoft Fabric via OneLake, allowing seamless access to curated data from Gold tables, enriched through ETL and machine learning workflows. This integration ensures that UMS business users can consume real-time, governed analytics directly from the Fabric environment. Enfrasys Solutions will configure Power BI connectivity within the Fabric workspace and establish initial datasets and dashboards. UMS is responsible for ongoing report development, user training, and access management. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: UMS AutoML Workflow Integration with Power BI. ## 3.3 Microsoft Entra ID: Identity and Access Management Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is a cloud-based identity and access management service that provides authentication, authorization, and directory services for Microsoft cloud platforms. It enables secure access to applications, enforces conditional access policies, and supports role-based access control (RBAC) across Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. Entra ID will be used to manage user identities, group memberships, and access permissions for the Fabric workspace, Power BI reports, and data resources. Enfrasys Solutions will configure initial Entra ID integration, RBAC policies, and workspace-level security. UMS shall maintain user lifecycle management, monitor access, and enforce organizational security standards. ## 3.4 Platform Management Responsibilities | Capability | Description | |---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Fabric Workspace Creation | Provisioning and initial configuration of Fabric workspace | | Data Factory Pipeline | Design and deployment of ETL workflows | | Synapse Analytics | Setup and optimization of big data analytics | | Data Science Workflow | Enablement of AutoML and manual ML processes | | Power BI Integration | Establishment of Power BI connectivity and initial dashboards | | Entra ID Security | Configuration of RBAC, authentication, and workspace access | | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------| | Fabric Workspace Setup | Create workspace, configure core services | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Data Pipeline Design | Develop ETL pipeline for SQL Server ingestion | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | ML Workflow Enablement | Configure AutoML and manual ML notebooks | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Power BI Dashboard Setup | Establish initial datasets and reports | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Entra ID Integration | Setup RBAC and workspace access policies | Enfrasys Solutions| Pending | | Workspace Governance | Manage users, permissions, and ongoing operations | UMS | Ongoing | | Report Development | Create and maintain business-aligned reports | UMS | Ongoing | | User Access Management | Maintain user lifecycle and monitor access | UMS | Ongoing | # 4.0 Network Design and Decision This section outlines the network topology and connectivity architecture for UMS, including secure on-premises to cloud data flows via the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG). ## 4.1 Network Connectivity Overview UMS's network design leverages a hybrid architecture, connecting on-premises data sources to Microsoft Fabric in the cloud. The primary data flow originates from the UMS Staging Area—specifically, the Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse—transiting securely through the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine before reaching UMS Fabric. All connections utilize outbound secure channels, eliminating the need for inbound ports and thereby reducing exposure to external threats. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_011_img_004.png) Figure 1: Data flow from UMS SQL Data Warehouse to UMS Fabric via OPDG. The OPDG acts as a bridge, facilitating secure data transfer from on-premises environments to Microsoft Fabric. All outbound traffic is governed by strict Azure Network Security Group (NSG) rules, which are referenced here and detailed in Section 6.0 Security Design. ## 4.2 Microsoft Fabric Data Gateway Architecture ### Microsoft Fabric Definition Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single cloud solution. It provides robust connectivity, security, and scalability for enterprise workloads, enabling seamless integration with on-premises and cloud data sources. ### On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) Overview The On-Premises Data Gateway is a Microsoft component that securely bridges on-premises data sources and cloud services such as Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. It enables secure outbound connectivity, allowing data transfer and authentication without exposing internal resources to inbound internet traffic. UMS shall implement the OPDG agent within a dedicated virtual machine, serving as the conduit between the Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse and Microsoft Fabric. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for the initial setup and configuration of the OPDG VM, while UMS shall manage ongoing operations, maintenance, and monitoring. ### Data Gateway VM Specifications | Component | Specification | |-------------------|-------------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ### Task/Migration Rollout | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------------| | OPDG VM Provisioning | Deploy VM with required OS and specs | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | OPDG Agent Installation | Install and configure OPDG agent | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Connectivity Validation | Test outbound secure connections to Microsoft Fabric | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Handover & Documentation | Transfer operational responsibility to UMS | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Ongoing Operations | Monitor, maintain, and update OPDG VM | UMS | Ongoing | ### Outbound Connectivity and Security All outbound connections from the OPDG VM are restricted to essential Microsoft endpoints, utilizing HTTPS (TCP port 443) for secure data transfer and authentication. Connectivity tests are permitted to Microsoft endpoints for network verification. Network security rules are referenced in this section and fully detailed in Section 6.0 Security Design. ## 4.3 Architecture Diagram ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ## 4.4 Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-----------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | On-premises to cloud data flows via OPDG | Adopted; ensures secure, outbound-only connectivity | | 2 | VM specification for OPDG | Windows 10/Server 2019, .NET 4.8, 8 vCPU, 8 GB RAM | | 3 | Outbound NSG rules for OPDG VM | Referenced; detailed in Security Design section | | 4 | Responsibility assignment | Enfrasys: setup; UMS: ongoing operations | # 5.0 Roles in Microsoft Fabric and Power BI This section defines formal roles, responsibilities, and access assignments for UMS personnel within Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments. ## 5.1 Microsoft Fabric Administrator Role Microsoft Fabric is a unified analytics platform from Microsoft that integrates data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single SaaS solution. Fabric enables organizations to build, manage, and govern data pipelines, lakehouses, warehouses, and Power BI reports within a secure, collaborative workspace. The Fabric Administrator is a privileged role responsible for overseeing the configuration, governance, and lifecycle management of Fabric workspaces. Administrators have the following capabilities: | Capability | Description | |-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Workspace Creation| Provision new Fabric workspaces and assign initial roles. | | Security Policy | Configure access controls, enforce compliance, and monitor workspace security. | | Resource Management| Manage datasets, pipelines, lakehouses, and integration with Power BI. | | Audit & Monitoring| Review activity logs, monitor usage, and respond to incidents. | | Role Assignment | Assign, update, and revoke workspace roles for UMS personnel. | Enfrasys Solutions shall perform the initial mapping and assignment of Fabric Administrator roles. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing management and access review. ## 5.2 Microsoft Fabric-native Roles Microsoft Fabric supports a range of native roles to facilitate collaboration and governance.
