UMS shall implement ongoing access management, including periodic reviews and updates to workspace roles. # 6.0 Microsoft Fabric and Power BI Design and Decision This section outlines the technical design, workflow, access, and governance decisions for UMS's implementation of Microsoft Fabric and Power BI. ## 6.1 Data Workflow Design Considerations Microsoft Fabric is an integrated analytics platform that brings together data engineering, data science, real-time analytics, and business intelligence in a unified SaaS environment. Power BI is a business analytics service that enables users to visualize data and share insights across an organization or embed them in an app or website. UMS's data workflow spans ingestion, cleansing, transformation, machine learning, and visualization. The following table summarizes key considerations: | ID | Description | Workload Type | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------| | DC01 | Data Pipeline will be used to ingest data from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Factory | | DC02 | The Bronze Lakehouse will store raw/original data ingested from Microsoft SQL Server. | Data Engineering | | DC03 | The Silver Lakehouse will store cleansed data sourced from the bronze Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC04 | The Gold Lakehouse will store transformed data sourced from the silver Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DC05 | Data cleansing and transformation for the Silver Lakehouse will be performed using Dataflow Gen2, Notebook, and Visual Query in the SQL Analytics Endpoint. | Data Engineering | | DC06 | Dataflow Gen2 will be used to move data between Lakehouses (e.g., from bronze to silver or silver to gold). | Data Factory | | DC07 | AutoML from Data Science will be used to demonstrate the Machine Learning Process, and the results will be stored in Gold Lakehouse | Data Science | | DC08 | Data modeling will be implemented in the Semantic Model, using data from the Gold Lakehouse. | Power BI | | DC09 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | | DE01 | The cleaned data stored in Silver Lakehouse will serve as the input for all machine learning experimentation. The chosen data will be one of the programs selected from the overall Staging DB. | Data Engineering | | DE02 | The Project will primarily adopt the Manual Machine Learning approach using Fabric Notebooks (PySpark or Python) to enable custom feature engineering, algorithm tuning, and model optimization. | Data Science | | DE03 | Users will perform model training, evaluation, and fine-tuning directly in the Notebook environment to achieve more accurate and explainable results tailored to UMS’s datasets. | Data Science | | DE04 | Trained models will be registered in the Fabric Model Registry to support model lifecycle management, versioning, and potential reuse across further adoption. | Data Science | | DE05 | Scoring Notebooks will be used to apply trained models to new or unseen data. The scored outputs, predictions, probabilities, and performance metrics will be stored in the Gold Lakehouse. | Data Engineering | | DE06 | The Gold Lakehouse will serve as the standardized repository for all modeled or scored results, ensuring consistency between AutoML and manual ML outputs. | Data Engineering | | DE07 | The Semantic Model will connect to the Gold Lakehouse via Direct Lake (OneLake) and Data Modelling will be implemented | Power BI | | DE08 | Power BI Desktop will connect to the Semantic Model via OneLake for data visualization. | Power BI | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_009_img_003.png) Figure 1: End-to-end data workflow architecture. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_021_img_005.png) Figure 2: AutoML and manual ML workflow integration. ## 6.2 Data Workflow Design Decisions UMS shall implement the following workflow design decisions, mapped to the considerations above: | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Data ingestion via Azure Data Factory from SQL Server (DC01) | Enfrasys Solutions will configure pipelines; UMS will maintain source connectivity. | | DD02 | Raw data storage in Bronze Lakehouse (DC02) | Enfrasys Solutions will provision Lakehouse; UMS will manage data retention policies. | | DD03 | Cleansing and transformation in Silver Lakehouse using Dataflow Gen2 and Notebooks (DC03, DC05, DE01) | Enfrasys Solutions will set up cleansing workflows; UMS will validate transformation logic.| | DD04 | Movement between Lakehouses via Dataflow Gen2 (DC06) | Enfrasys Solutions will automate dataflow triggers; UMS will monitor data lineage. | | DD05 | Machine Learning via AutoML and manual notebooks, storing results in Gold Lakehouse (DC07, DE02-DE06) | Enfrasys Solutions will implement model registry and scoring; UMS will govern model lifecycle.| | DD06 | Semantic Model connection and Power BI visualization via OneLake (DC08, DC09, DE07, DE08) | Enfrasys Solutions will configure semantic models; UMS will develop reports and dashboards.| ## 6.3 Access Design Considerations and Decisions Microsoft Entra ID is the cloud-based identity and access management service for Azure and Microsoft 365, providing authentication, authorization, and security controls such as Conditional Access and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). ### 6.3.1 Conditional Access Due to licensing constraints, UMS is not eligible for Conditional Access at this stage. MFA is enforced at the tenant level to secure access. | No. | Policy | Decision | |------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | DD01 | Configure Policy in Conditional Access to include Power BI Service, Azure Data Explorer, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Storage | Deferred until UMS upgrades license; MFA enforced as interim measure. | | DD02 | Include UMS user inside Conditional Access policy | Deferred; MFA covers all UMS users. | | DD03 | UMS users go through additional verification, MFA, to reduce unexpected login prompts | Enfrasys Solutions will configure MFA; UMS will manage user onboarding. | | DD04 | Users able to access Microsoft Fabric’s environment | Enfrasys Solutions will enable access via MFA; UMS will monitor access logs. | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 3: Conditional access workflow. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 4: Entra authentication and access enforcement. ### 6.3.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) UMS shall enforce MFA for all users accessing Microsoft Fabric and Power BI resources. Enfrasys Solutions is responsible for initial configuration; UMS is responsible for ongoing user management. ## 6.4 Virtual Machine (VM) Size The following VM specifications are recommended for gateway and supporting components: | Component | Specification | |-------------------|----------------------------------------------| | Operating System | Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows Server 2019 (64-bit) | | .NET Framework | Version 4.8 | | vCPU | 8 cores | | RAM | Minimum 8 GB | ## 6.5 Resource Organization Design ### 6.5.1 Management Groups Management groups in Azure provide a hierarchical structure for organizing resources and applying governance policies. Enfrasys Solutions recommends that UMS establish a root management group for all Fabric-related resources, with child management groups for staging, production, and sandbox environments. This structure enables consistent policy enforcement and cost tracking. ### 6.5.2 Naming and Tagging #### Azure Naming Convention | Resource | Abbreviation | Example | |------------------|-------------|--------------------------| | Resource Group | RG | Fabric-RG | | Storage Account | SA | umsdatastorage01 | | Virtual Machine | VM | ums-fabric-vm01 | #### Fabric Naming Convention | No. | Item in Fabric | Naming Convention | |-----|---------------------|----------------------------------------| | 1 | Fabric Capacity | stagingareadevcapacity01 | | 2 | Workspace | StudentAffairs_dev_WS | | 3 | Lakehouse | StudentAffairs_Bronze_LK
StudentAffairs_Silver_LK | | 4 | Dataflow | StudentAffairs_Bronze_to_Silver_DF
StudentAffairs_Silver_to_Gold_DF | | 5 | Semantic Model | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_SM | | 6 | Pipeline | StudentAffairs_Ingest_Data_PL | | 7 | Report | StudentAffairs_StudentPerformance_Report | #### Acronyms | Abbreviation | Description | |--------------|----------------------| | LK | Lakehouse | | DF | Dataflow | | SM | Semantic Model | | PL | Pipeline | | WS | Workspace | ## 6.6 Governance Consideration ### 6.6.1 Governance Disciplines UMS shall adopt governance disciplines for cost management and compliance. Enfrasys Solutions will configure initial policies; UMS will be responsible for ongoing reviews. | Cost Item | Owner | Review Frequency | |-------------------------|---------------|------------------| | Azure Resource Usage | UMS | Monthly | | Power BI Licensing | UMS | Quarterly | | Fabric Workspace Costs | UMS | Monthly | | VM and Gateway Costs | UMS | Monthly | ### 6.6.2 Violation Triggers and Actions | Trigger | Action | Owner | |------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|---------------| | Unauthorized access detected | Immediate account review and MFA reset | UMS | | Cost overrun threshold breached | Alert and resource usage audit | UMS | | Non-compliant resource naming/tagging | Resource remediation and policy update | UMS | | License expiry or downgrade | License renewal and access review | UMS | # 7.0 Security Design and Decision This section details the security architecture for UMS's OPDG and Fabric environment, including network security group (NSG) rules and encryption strategies. ## 7.1 Azure NSG Overview Azure Network Security Groups (NSGs) are a fundamental security feature in Microsoft Azure, providing granular control over inbound and outbound network traffic to Azure resources. NSGs use rules to allow or deny traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol, ensuring only authorized communications occur within the environment. Enfrasys Solutions shall configure initial NSG rules for the OPDG and Fabric environment. UMS shall be responsible for ongoing monitoring and compliance of these rules. ### Outbound NSG Rules for OPDG and Fabric Environment | Name | Priority | Source | Source Ports | Destination | Destination Ports | Protocol | Access | |----------------------------|----------|-----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|----------|--------| | Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services | 130 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.servicebus.windows.net, *.download.microsoft.com, *.core.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com, *.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com, *.analysis.windows.net | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Authentication | 140 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.login.windows.net, login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msauth.net, aadcdn.msftauth.net, *.microsoftonline-p.com, login.live.com | 443 | TCP | Allow | | Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test| 160 | VM Private IP Addresses| * | *.msftncsi.com | 80 | TCP | Allow | ## 7.2 Encryption Design Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, Azure Data Explorer, and Azure SQL Database all leverage industry-standard encryption mechanisms to protect data both at rest and in transit. - **Encryption at Rest:** All data stored within Microsoft Fabric, associated Azure services, and OPDG is protected using Azure Storage Service Encryption (SSE) and Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for SQL workloads. This ensures that data is encrypted using AES-256 or equivalent algorithms, preventing unauthorized access to stored information. - **Encryption in Transit:** Data flows between OPDG, Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and Azure services utilize SSL/TLS protocols (minimum TLS 1.2) for all communications. Outbound NSG rules explicitly permit only HTTPS (TCP port 443) and HTTP (TCP port 80) traffic to Microsoft endpoints, ensuring secure transmission of authentication, data, and connectivity information. Enfrasys Solutions shall implement initial encryption configurations and validate secure data flows. UMS shall monitor encryption compliance and maintain audit records for all sensitive workloads. ## 7.3 Security Configuration Task Assignment | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |----------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------|---------| | NSG Rule Deployment | Create and apply outbound NSG rules for OPDG and Fabric environment | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Encryption Configuration | Enable SSE, TDE, and enforce TLS for all relevant Azure and Fabric services | Enfrasys Solutions | Pending | | Security Monitoring | Monitor NSG rule compliance, encryption status, and maintain audit logs | UMS | Ongoing | ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_006_img_001.png) Figure 1: Conditional access enforcement architecture. ![](C:\Users\MuhammadIdzhansKhair\OneDrive - ENFRASYS\Documents\project-internal\design-document-converter\python-backend-development\outputs\api_uploads\c31373d8-0775-439f-8d34-7ec0a38cacc5\images\slide_006_img_002.png) Figure 2: Authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric. ## 7.4 Security Design Decisions | No. | Design Decision | Decision | |-----|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------| | SD01| Outbound NSG rules restrict OPDG VM traffic to Microsoft endpoints only | Enforced | | SD02| Encryption at rest enabled for all Azure and Fabric data stores | Enforced | | SD03| Encryption in transit enforced via HTTPS/TLS for all external communications | Enforced | | SD04| Enfrasys Solutions responsible for initial security setup; UMS for ongoing compliance | Assigned | # 8.0 Deployment & Migration Approach This section outlines the structured approach for deployment and migration of the Microsoft Fabric solution for UMS, including preparatory activities, pilot execution, production rollout, and post-migration support. ## 8.1 Pre-Migration/Setup Enfrasys Solutions and UMS shall jointly execute preparatory tasks to ensure readiness for migration and deployment. The following table details the activities, planned start dates, responsible parties, and current status. | No | Activities | Planned Start | Action By | Status | Remarks | |----|------------|---------------|-----------|--------|---------| | 1 | Deliver Design Document to be reviewed by UMS & Sign Off | Fri, 7-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | | 2 | Confirmation on the new server (data warehouse copy – Staging Area DB) to be ingested | Mon, 10-Nov-25 | UMS | Not-Started | | | 3 | Fabric Environment Setup | Wed, 12-Nov-25 | Enfrasys | Not-Started | | | 4 | Install On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) in VM to establish Data Gateway | Mon, 17-Nov-25 | Enfrasys
UMS | Not-Started | | ## 8.2 Pilot Enfrasys Solutions recommends a pilot deployment to validate the Microsoft Fabric environment and associated data workflows prior to full production rollout. ### Pilot Deployment Approach - Enfrasys Solutions will provision a controlled Fabric workspace and configure connectivity to the staging area database. - UMS shall provide test datasets and validate ingestion, transformation, and reporting workflows. - Pilot users from UMS will access reports and dashboards to ensure data accuracy and performance. ### Pilot Acceptance Criteria - Successful ingestion of sample datasets from the staging area. - Data refresh and transformation processes complete without errors. - Reports and dashboards render correctly and reflect accurate data. - No critical issues identified by UMS pilot users. ## 8.3 Production UMS shall implement the production rollout in structured migration waves, following a formal timeline to minimize disruption and ensure operational continuity. ### Production Rollout Timeline | Item | Activities | Action By | Status | |------|------------|-----------|--------| | D-7 | Finalize Fabric workspace configuration and security settings | Enfrasys | Pending | | D-7 | Confirm production dataset readiness | UMS | Pending | | D-3 | Conduct user training and distribute access guides | Enfrasys | Pending | | D-1 | Execute pre-cutover validation of data pipelines | Enfrasys
UMS | Pending | | D0 | Cutover: Switch data source connections to production | Enfrasys | Pending | | D0 | UMS business users validate live reports | UMS | Pending | | D+1 | Monitor data refreshes and resolve post-cutover issues | Enfrasys | Pending | | D+3 | Complete post-migration review and sign-off | UMS | Pending | ## 8.4 Post-Migration/Support Enfrasys Solutions shall provide post-migration support and facilitate handover to UMS for ongoing operations. ### Support Handover - Enfrasys Solutions will deliver comprehensive documentation covering Fabric configuration, data gateway setup, and operational procedures. - UMS personnel will be briefed on day-to-day management, monitoring, and troubleshooting. ### Ongoing Support Responsibilities - Enfrasys Solutions: Provide Tier 2 support for Fabric environment, resolve escalated issues, and assist with periodic reviews. - UMS: Manage routine operations, monitor data pipelines, and escalate complex incidents to Enfrasys Solutions as required. # 9.0 Appendix The following appendix sections provide reference links and boilerplate content for UMS, covering Microsoft and Enfrasys Solutions best practices across computing, networking, identity & security, logging & monitoring, cloud governance, and Microsoft Fabric & Power BI. ## 9.1 Appendix 1 Computing ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Compute Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/) Title: [Azure VM Deployment Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/deployment-methods) Title: [Enfrasys Solutions Computing Standards](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/cloud-infrastructure/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure VM Size Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sizes) Title: [Azure Subscription and Resource Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-subscription-service-limits) Title: [Azure Disk Storage Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-types) ### Others Title: [Azure Availability Sets and Zones](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/availability-set-overview) Title: [Azure Reserved VM Instances](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/reservations/save-compute-costs-reserved-vm-instance) Title: [Azure Compute SLA](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/sla) ## 9.2 Appendix 2 Network ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Networking Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/networking/) Title: [Azure Virtual Network Design](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview) Title: [Enfrasys Network Architecture Best Practices](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/cloud-networking/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure Networking Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-network-limits) Title: [Azure ExpressRoute and VPN Gateway Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/expressroute-limits) Title: [Azure NSG Rules Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/network-security-groups-overview) ### Others Title: [Azure Network Security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/network-security) Title: [Azure Load Balancer Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/load-balancer-overview) Title: [Azure DNS Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/dns/dns-overview) ## 9.3 Appendix 3 Identity & Security ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Microsoft Entra ID Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/overview-what-is-azure-ad) Title: [Azure Security Center Introduction](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-intro) Title: [Enfrasys Identity and Security Best Practices](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/cloud-security/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Entra ID Service Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-users/service-limits-azure-ad) Title: [Azure Role-Based Access Control Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/rbac-permissions) Title: [Azure Security Policy Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts) ### Others Title: [Azure MFA Deployment Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/authentication/howto-mfa-getstarted) Title: [Azure Security Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/best-practices) Title: [Azure Identity Protection](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/identity-protection/overview-identity-protection) ## 9.4 Appendix 4 Logging & Monitoring ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Monitor Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/overview) Title: [Log Analytics Workspace Introduction](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview) Title: [Enfrasys Monitoring Best Practices](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/cloud-monitoring/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure Monitor Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/limits) Title: [Log Analytics Workspace Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/log-analytics-workspace-overview#limits) Title: [Azure Diagnostic Settings Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/diagnostic-settings) ### Others Title: [Azure Alerting and Action Groups](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/alerts/alerts-overview) Title: [Azure Monitoring Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/best-practices) Title: [Azure Application Insights](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/app/app-insights-overview) ## 9.5 Appendix 5 Cloud Governance ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Azure Governance Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/) Title: [Azure Management Groups and Subscriptions](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview) Title: [Enfrasys Cloud Governance Framework](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/cloud-governance/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Azure Policy and Blueprint Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts) Title: [Management Group and Subscription Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/management-groups/overview#limits) Title: [Azure Cost Management Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cost-management-billing/cost-management-billing-overview) ### Others Title: [Azure Resource Naming Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cloud-adoption-framework/ready/azure-best-practices/naming-and-tagging) Title: [Azure Tagging Strategy](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/tag-resources) Title: [Azure Governance Checklist](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/checklist) ## 9.6 Appendix 6 Microsoft Fabric & Power BI ### Introduction/Prerequisites Title: [Microsoft Fabric Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/overview/) Title: [Power BI Service Introduction](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/fundamentals/service-overview) Title: [Enfrasys Data Analytics Best Practices](https://www.enfrasys.com/solutions/data-analytics/) ### Limits and Boundaries Title: [Microsoft Fabric Capacity Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/capacity/capacity-limits) Title: [Power BI Dataset Size Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-dataset-size) Title: [Power BI Workspace Limits](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-workspace-limits) ### Others Title: [Microsoft Fabric Security](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/security/security-overview) Title: [Power BI Governance Best Practices](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security) Title: [Power BI Integration with Azure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/service-azure-data-lake-gen2)

asset_library.description:
The diagram illustrates a technical architecture involving Azure components and user access management. 

1. **Fabric-RG**: This represents a Resource Group in Azure, which is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It is depicted with an icon suggesting a grouping or management function.

2. **UMS Fabric**: This component likely represents a service or application layer within the Azure environment, possibly related to user management or a specific application fabric.

3. **Conditional Access**: This is a security feature in Azure Active Directory that provides automated access control decisions for accessing cloud apps based on conditions. It is depicted with a shield and lock icon, indicating security enforcement.

4. **External User**: This represents users outside the organization who need access to the resources or services provided by the UMS Fabric. 

**Data Flow and Connections**:
- The arrow indicates a data flow or access request from the External User to the UMS Fabric.
- The Conditional Access component acts as a gatekeeper, enforcing security policies before allowing the External User to access the UMS Fabric.
- The entire setup is encapsulated within the Fabric-RG, suggesting that all these components are managed within a single Azure Resource Group for organizational and management purposes.

The diagram illustrates an authentication and access control workflow for Microsoft Fabric using Entra conditional access policies.

1. **User Authentication Attempt**: The process begins with a user attempting to authenticate to Microsoft Fabric.

2. **Entra Conditional Access Policies**: The authentication request is evaluated against Entra conditional access policies.

3. **Signal Collection**: Signals are gathered, such as user and group information and network location.

4. **Decision Making**: Based on the collected signals, a decision is made regarding access.

5. **Enforcement**: The decision is enforced, determining whether the user is granted or denied access.

6. **Microsoft Fabric Access**: If access is granted, the user can interact with Microsoft Fabric and its components, including OneLake and various services depicted by icons.

This workflow ensures secure and conditional access to Microsoft Fabric resources.

The diagram represents a data processing and analytics architecture within the UMS Fabric environment. The workflow begins with data ingestion from a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, labeled as "UMS – Staging Area." This data is ingested into the system using Azure Data Factory.

The ingested data is first stored in the "XX_Bronze_LK Tables," which likely serve as the raw data storage layer. The data then progresses to the "XX_Silver_LK Tables," indicating a refined or cleansed data layer. This transition is also managed by Data Factory.

Subsequently, the data is processed further, possibly involving transformation or enrichment, and stored in the "XX_Gold_LK Tables." This layer represents the curated, business-ready data. The Data Science component is involved at this stage, suggesting the application of advanced analytics or machine learning models.

The "Identity_SM" component is integrated at the Gold layer, potentially for identity management or security purposes.

Finally, the processed data is connected via OneLake to Power BI for visualization and business intelligence purposes, enabling end-users to derive insights from the data.

The diagram illustrates a data flow architecture involving three main components: a Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, an On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG), and UMS Fabric.

1. **UMS Staging Area - Microsoft SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse**: This component represents the on-premises data storage system where data is initially staged. It is depicted as a database icon, indicating its role in storing and managing structured data.

2. **On-Premise Data Gateway (OPDG)**: This component acts as a bridge between the on-premises data warehouse and the cloud-based UMS Fabric. It facilitates secure data transfer without requiring inbound ports, ensuring that data can be securely transmitted outwards to the cloud service. The OPDG is crucial for maintaining a secure connection while allowing data to flow from the on-premises environment to the cloud.

3. **UMS Fabric**: This represents the cloud-based component that receives data from the on-premises data warehouse via the OPDG. The UMS Fabric is likely a part of a larger cloud service platform, designed to process, analyze, or further distribute the data received.

The data flow begins at the SQL Server 2017 Data Warehouse, moves through the OPDG, and reaches the UMS Fabric. The connection between the OPDG and UMS Fabric is described as an "Outbound Secure Connection," emphasizing security and the absence of the need for inbound ports, which enhances the security posture by minimizing exposure to external threats.

The diagram illustrates a data processing and machine learning workflow within an Azure environment, integrating UMS Fabric and OneLake.

1. **Data Source**: 
   - **XX_Silver_LK Tables**: Acts as the initial data source stored in OneLake.

2. **Machine Learning Processes**:
   - **AutoML**: 
     - Data from XX_Silver_LK Tables is fed into an AutoML Experiment.
     - The experiment results are scored using a notebook, and the output is directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.
   - **Manual Machine Learning**:
     - Alternatively, data from XX_Silver_LK Tables can be processed through Manual Machine Learning.
     - Modelling is performed using a notebook, with results also directed to XX_Gold_LK Tables.

3. **Data Storage and Identity Management**:
   - **XX_Gold_LK Tables**: Stores the processed and scored data.
   - **Identity_SM**: Manages identity-related data or processes, linked with XX_Gold_LK Tables.

4. **Data Visualization**:
   - **Power BI**: Connected via OneLake to XX_Gold_LK Tables for data visualization and reporting.

This architecture supports both automated and manual machine learning workflows, with seamless integration into data visualization tools for comprehensive analysis and reporting.

slides.title: Azure Outbound Security Rules Consideration
slides.notes:
defines the outbound network rules required to allow the On-Premises Data Gateway (OPDG) virtual machine (VM) to securely connect to Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, and related Microsoft cloud services. These rules ensure that the gateway can authenticate, communicate, and transfer data through HTTPS in compliance with Azure networking best practices.

Rule Name : Allow_OPDG_HTTPS_Services
Purpose: Enables HTTPS communication from the gateway VM to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI endpoints.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound TCP traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) from VM Private IP Addresses to essential Microsoft domains, including Fabric, Power BI, and Azure core services (e.g., *.fabric.microsoft.com, *.powerbi.com). It ensures that the gateway can send and receive data securely using SSL/TLS encryption.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Authentication
Purpose: Enables authentication and identity verification with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD).
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTPS traffic (TCP port 443) to identity and login domains such as login.microsoftonline.com, aadcdn.msftauth.net, and login.windows.net. It enables user sign-ins, token validation, and secure connection establishment with Microsoft cloud services.

Rule Name: Allow_OPDG_Connectivity_Test
Purpose: Supports network connectivity verification for the OPDG service.
Explanation: This rule allows outbound HTTP traffic on port 80 (TCP) to *.msftncsi.com, a Microsoft endpoint used for network connectivity status indication and troubleshooting. It ensures the VM can verify external internet access and service availability.
